confirmedCritHigh.title: Committed '*-change-me' JWT/SIGNING/INTERNAL secrets default under NODE_ENV=production (forge admin tokens)
confirmedCritHigh.line: api: JWT_SECRET ~ '${JWT_SECRET:-openagentic-dev-jwt-secret-change-me}', SIGNING_SECRET '...-signing-secret-change-me', INTERNAL_API_KEY '...-internal-key-change-me'; mirrored in workflows + mcp-proxy (JWT_SECRET/SIGNING_SECRET/API_INTERNAL_KEY/INTERNAL_API_KEY) and ui FRONTEND_SECRET 'dev-frontend-secret-change-in-prod'
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: Make these required with no default under production: use ${JWT_SECRET:?set JWT_SECRET in .env} (same pattern already used for POSTGRES_PASSWORD), and add a boot-time guard that refuses to start when NODE_ENV=production and any of JWT_SECRET/SIGNING_SECRET/INTERNAL_API_KEY is unset OR matches a known weak literal (substring 'change-me'/'dev-'/'changeme'). Generate strong random values in install.sh/wizard and write them to .env.

confirmedCritHigh.title: mcp-proxy grants system-admin to unauthenticated callers and system-root to any 'oa_sys_' token with no signature/DB check
confirmedCritHigh.line: ~636 (no credentials -> user_id='system-admin', is_admin=True); ~669 (token.startswith('oa_sys_') -> user_id='system-root', is_admin=True, SP creds) — both execute before any ENABLE_AUTH check; RBAC at ~1050 keys on is_admin which both bypasses satisfy; only backstop MCP_READ_ONLY_MODE defaults false (~98)
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: Fail closed: when ENABLE_AUTH is true, reject requests with no credentials (401) instead of returning system-admin. Validate 'oa_sys_' tokens the same way the api does — bcrypt against the api-keys table (or call /api/auth/validate-token / /api/auth/me) — never trust the prefix alone. Require the proxy to authenticate the api caller via the shared INTERNAL_API_KEY constant-time compared, and remove the unconditional admin grant. Default MCP_READ_ONLY_MODE to true is a secondary defense, not the fix.

confirmedCritHigh.title: secrets.config.ts is fail-open: auto-generates ephemeral secrets under production and its placeholder blocklist misses the actual shipped 'openagentic-dev-*-change-me' default
confirmedCritHigh.line: validateSecret() ~63-118 ('never crashes', generates random on missing/placeholder, only console.error); exactPlaceholders matches 'change-me' as EXACT only (~85) and substringPlaceholders (~95) lacks 'change-me'/'dev-', so 'openagentic-dev-jwt-secret-change-me' passes; consumed by startup/01-secrets.ts which is critical:false and catches errors
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: Under NODE_ENV=production, fail CLOSED (throw and abort boot) when a required secret is missing or matches a weak pattern; add 'change-me' and 'dev-' to the substring blocklist; make startup/01-secrets.ts critical:true in production. Keep the auto-generate behavior only for NODE_ENV=development.

confirmedCritHigh.title: Unauthenticated SSRF via POST /api/setup/probe-ollama (no egress guard, no IP-range block, not needsSetup-gated)
confirmedCritHigh.line: probe-ollama handler ~58-78: fetch(`${host}/api/tags`) on attacker-controlled host with no validation; registered unconditionally via plugins/setup.plugin.ts (no setup-needed gate, unlike /complete which guards on existing admin)
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: Gate probe-ollama behind a needsSetup check (refuse once an admin exists), validate the host against an allowlist / block RFC1918, link-local 169.254.169.254 (cloud metadata), loopback, and non-http(s) schemes, and resolve+pin the IP before fetch to prevent DNS-rebinding. Long term, require the magic-boot token like /complete.

confirmedCritHigh.title: Publicly-known *-change-me JWT/SIGNING/INTERNAL secrets used raw by auth code on a bare `docker compose up`
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: These defaults (openagentic-dev-jwt-secret-change-me / -signing-secret-change-me / -internal-key-change-me) are world-readable in the repo. api auth code reads process.env.JWT_SECRET RAW (tokenValidator.ts:32, auth.ts:606+, googleAuth.ts:307, chat/middleware/auth.middleware.ts:178, ChatMCPService.ts:39) — secrets.config.ts placeholder-detection is never written back to process.env, so a documented `docker compose up -d` (README/CONTRIBUTING quickstart) runs with forgeable secrets: an attacker signs an admin JWT, and sends `Bearer openagentic-dev-internal-key-change-me` to mcp-proxy /call (plain match at main.py:716) → is_admin SP-cred cloud tool exec. Fix: remove all `:-...change-me` and helm plaintext defaults; require these vars (`${VAR:?set in .env}`) so a bare up fails fast; add a NODE_ENV=production boot guard that refuses to start when any of JWT_SECRET/SIGNING_SECRET/INTERNAL_API_KEY is unset OR matches a known placeholder, and make that the SAME guard for api + mcp-proxy + workflows.

confirmedCritHigh.title: mcp-proxy grants system-admin on no creds and system-root on unsigned 'oa_sys_' prefix (auth bypass)
confirmedCritHigh.nist: AC-3, AC-6, IA-2, IA-5, SC-13, AU-2
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: get_user_info() is the Depends() guard on every tool endpoint (/mcp, /mcp/tool, /call, /batch-call). `if not credentials:` returns is_admin:True system-admin, and `token.startswith('oa_sys_')` returns is_admin:True system-root with NO signature/secret verification — any `Bearer oa_sys_<anything>` is accepted. Neither path is gated by ENABLE_AUTH (which only gates Azure OAuth init at boot). Fix: when ENABLE_AUTH, reject missing credentials with 401; replace the prefix check with an HMAC verification of the system token against SIGNING_SECRET (the api already mints it via mintInterServiceSystemToken HMAC-SHA256 — verify it, do not trust the prefix); keep the empty-secret→prefix-only path rejected.

confirmedCritHigh.title: Unauthenticated SSRF via POST /api/setup/probe-ollama (no setup-gate, no egress guard)
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: The route is intentionally unauthenticated, is NOT gated on needsSetup/hasAdmin (stays live after setup), and does fetch(`${host}/api/tags`) on attacker-controlled host with no private-IP/metadata denylist (a deny_if_private SSRF guard already exists in oap-web-mcp/tests but is not applied here). Error bodies reflect target status/failure → blind internal port-scan + cloud-metadata (169.254.169.254) reach. Fix: gate the route on `needsSetup === true` (return 409 once an admin exists), and resolve+validate `host` against an allowlist / deny RFC1918+link-local+metadata before fetch using the existing SSRF guard.

confirmedCritHigh.title: FedRAMP remediation docs SHIP in public repo and re-leak the exact PII/IP/tenant they document removing
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: P0-preserve-classification.json + ledger/P0-preserve-hardening.md ship on launch and quote verbatim, in their reason/evidence fields: personal email <REDACTED-PERSONAL-EMAIL>; real person email <REDACTED-PERSONAL-EMAIL>; developer public IP <REDACTED-PUBLIC-IP> + LAN subnets <REDACTED-LAN-SUBNET> and <REDACTED-LAN-SUBNET>; real Azure AD tenant/account <REDACTED-TEST-ACCOUNT>; internal hostname <REDACTED-INTERNAL-HOST>. The source files were correctly scrubbed but the audit evidence re-introduced every leak. Fix: remove docs/fedramp-remediation/ from the public/launch tree (move to a private compliance store) OR redact all literal PII/IP/tenant/hostname values to placeholders before launch.

confirmedCritHigh.title: mcp-proxy README states wrong license (MIT vs repo Apache-2.0) + fabricated/stale references
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: Line 818 'MIT License - see LICENSE' contradicts the repo's actual Apache-2.0 LICENSE — a legal/licensing misstatement that gets screenshotted on launch. Also fabricated: `github.com/your-org/openagentic` placeholder (219,825), `helm/openagenticchat-v3` chart path that does not exist (real path helm/openagentic; 259,684), a non-existent team email mcp-proxy-team@openagentic.io (827), and an unverified docs URL (824). Fix: change to Apache-2.0; replace your-org with agentic-work; fix the helm path to helm/openagentic; remove the fabricated team email + unverified docs link or point to real ones.

confirmedCritHigh.title: Internal JWT/SIGNING/INTERNAL_API_KEY default to publicly-known '*-change-me' strings under NODE_ENV=production (forge-admin-token)
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: getJWTSecret/getSigningSecret read process.env.JWT_SECRET||SIGNING_SECRET DIRECTLY (bypassing secrets.config.ts validateSecret), and the compose `:-openagentic-dev-jwt-secret-change-me` defaults mean an operator who doesn't override them signs/validates internal JWTs with a string committed to the public repo. validateSecret would not even catch it: its substringPlaceholders list is only your_secret_here/replace_me/placeholder, NOT '-change-me'. Remove the insecure compose defaults (require the var, fail boot if unset under production) and add '-change-me'/'dev-jwt-secret'/'dev-internal-key' to the secrets.config.ts blocklist so the value is rejected at boot, not silently trusted.

confirmedCritHigh.title: mcp-proxy get_user_info: any token starting 'oa_sys_' -> system-root admin with NO signature check; missing creds -> system-admin; neither gated by ENABLE_AUTH
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: get_user_info() is the auth dependency for /call, tools, and session endpoints (lines 968/1329/1495/...). A request with no Authorization header returns is_admin:true (system-admin), and any token literally prefixed 'oa_sys_' returns is_admin:true (system-root) with zero HMAC/signature verification. ENABLE_AUTH only gates Azure OAuth init (line 292), NOT this function, so both bypasses fire even with ENABLE_AUTH=true. Verify oa_sys_ tokens against a server-side secret/HMAC, and when ENABLE_AUTH is true reject missing-credential requests instead of granting admin.

confirmedCritHigh.title: POST /api/setup/probe-ollama is unauthenticated with no egress/SSRF guard and is not setup-gated
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: The route (explicitly commented 'intentionally UNAUTHENTICATED') accepts an arbitrary `host` and does fetch(`${host}/api/tags`) with no allowlist and no needsSetup gate, so post-setup it remains a blind SSRF primitive against internal services / cloud IMDS. Gate it behind GET /api/setup/status.needsSetup (refuse once an admin exists) and restrict the target to loopback/host.docker.internal/private-range Ollama hosts only.

confirmedCritHigh.title: clusterPlugin (/api/cluster/services) is never registered -> navigable docs 'Deployed Services' page is permanently broken (404)
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: clusterPlugin serving GET /api/cluster/services exists but is never imported/registered in server.ts (grep finds zero registrations). The docs page id 'deployed-services' (title 'Deployed Services', 'All HTTP endpoints' sibling) polls /api/cluster/services every 30s and stays in a permanent error banner for every deployment. Either register clusterPlugin in server.ts (note its k8s loadFromCluster() means it only returns data under helm, not compose) or remove the dead page from the docs nav.

confirmedCritHigh.title: Docs 'API Routes' page advertises fabricated endpoints that have no handler (/api/chat/completions, /api/conversations*, /api/flows*, /api/mcp/invoke)
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: The navigable docs page (id 'api-routes', renders alongside live Swagger) hand-lists endpoints that do not exist: POST /api/chat/completions(+/cancel) (real path is /api/chat/stream), GET/DELETE/PATCH /api/conversations[/:id] (no such route; sessions live under /api/chat), GET/POST /api/flows + /api/flows/:id/execute + /runs (flows are served under /api/workflows, no /api/flows alias), and POST /api/mcp/invoke (real is /api/mcp/tools/execute). Correct the curated table to match the real routes (or drive it from the OpenAPI/Swagger spec it already renders).

confirmedCritHigh.title: WorkflowExecutionEngine.ts forked across two services and already diverged (4029 vs 5935 lines, ~64% similar) — both LIVE
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: Complete the documented migration to executeViaWorkflowsService: re-point the remaining legacy call-sites (embed.ts:104 dynamic import, WorkflowTestRunner.ts:362 new WorkflowExecutionEngine, routes/workflows.ts abortWorkflowExecution) at the workflows-svc proxy, confirm the workflow_local_engine_fallback_total counter reads zero, then DELETE services/openagentic-api/src/services/WorkflowExecutionEngine.ts so the workflows-svc copy is the single source of truth.

confirmedCritHigh.title: WorkflowCompiler.ts forked across api and workflows, diverged (678 vs 983 lines, ~69% similar) — both LIVE
confirmedCritHigh.line: imported at routes/workflows.ts:56 + routes/v1/webhooks.ts:26 (api) and workflows index.ts (svc)
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: Move WorkflowCompiler into the shared @openagentic/workflow-engine package (like the node registry) and replace both copies with re-export shims, or fold the api copy into the executeViaWorkflowsService migration so only the workflows-svc compiles graphs.

confirmedCritHigh.title: WorkflowSecretService.ts forked across api and workflows (642 vs 601 lines, 89% similar) — security-sensitive secret-handling logic duplicated and diverging
confirmedCritHigh.line: api importers: admin-workflow-secrets.ts, DataSourceService.ts, WorkflowCompiler.ts, WorkflowExecutionEngine.ts
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: Two copies of secret resolution/redaction/ACL enforcement is a FedRAMP risk: a fix to one (e.g. a redaction or scoping bug) can silently leave the other vulnerable. Promote WorkflowSecretService into the shared workflow-engine package as the single authority and replace both with shims.

confirmedCritHigh.title: [re-confirm #3] mcp-proxy grants is_admin/system-root on the 'oa_sys_' string prefix with NO signature check, and grants system-admin when NO credentials are sent (not ENABLE_AUTH-gated)
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: STILL REAL. get_user_info returns is_admin:true for any request with no Authorization header AND for any token literally prefixed 'oa_sys_' — no HMAC/signature/shared-secret comparison. Replace the prefix check with a constant-time compare against a real signed system token (or the INTERNAL_API_KEY path already present), and gate the no-credentials branch behind ENABLE_AUTH so it cannot grant admin in production.

confirmedCritHigh.title: [re-confirm #2] POST /api/setup/probe-ollama is unauthenticated SSRF — fetches attacker-controlled host with no egress guard and no setup-gating
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: STILL REAL. Unlike /complete (which is magicToken-gated), /probe-ollama has no preHandler auth and no setup-gating, and fetches an arbitrary request.body.host — a server-side request forgery into internal networks/cloud metadata. Add the same magic-token/setup-only gate plus an SSRF egress allowlist (block private/link-local/metadata ranges) before the fetch.

confirmedCritHigh.title: [re-confirm #1] docker-compose.yml defaults JWT_SECRET / SIGNING_SECRET / INTERNAL_API_KEY to '*-change-me' literals usable under NODE_ENV=production
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: STILL REAL. The :- fallbacks bake known constant secrets that forge internal JWTs/admin tokens if .env is missing. Remove the insecure defaults (require the vars, e.g. ${JWT_SECRET:?set JWT_SECRET}) or fail boot when NODE_ENV=production and any of these equal the *-change-me literal.

confirmedCritHigh.title: 25 unreferenced .jpeg design/screenshot dumps committed in repo ROOT
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: Delete all 25 root .jpeg files (verified 0 references via git grep). Add `*.jpeg`/`*.jpg` (or a `/*.jpeg` root anchor) to .gitignore alongside the existing `*.png` rule so screenshot dumps cannot re-leak. They leak the private registry hostname `<REDACTED-INTERNAL-REGISTRY>` and a pinned internal tag, and cannot be built without VPN/registry access.

confirmedCritHigh.title: Internal-only working docs leak dev home paths, private registry creds, internal IPs/hostnames, and proprietary enterprise architecture

These are internal working notes: /home/trent/... home paths, <REDACTED-INTERNAL-REGISTRY> admin docker-login commands, internal IPs (<REDACTED-INTERNAL-IP>) + node names (dalek/data/hal/k8a-e) + namespaces (<REDACTED-INTERNAL-NS>/open-dev), the proprietary 'agenticwork-ui/api' brand + closed enterprise internals (AwTool, AuthorizationSpine, controlPlaneAuthz), and instructions to preserve the removed Code Mode subsystem. Add tests/demos/*.md to the sync-upstream SKIP list so they cannot re-leak.

confirmedCritHigh.title: mcp-proxy README declares MIT license; repo is Apache-2.0
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: Change 'MIT License - see [LICENSE]' to 'Apache-2.0 - see [LICENSE](../../LICENSE)' to match root LICENSE, README badge, CONTRIBUTING, and SECURITY which all state Apache-2.0.

confirmedCritHigh.line: 176-260 (API), ~258/660 (helm/openagenticchat-v3), 600-660 (Project Structure files), 296-312 (env vars), 178-230/740-770 (mcp_* metrics + notify_admin), 815-830 (Support block)

confirmedCritHigh.title: Systematic 14-vs-9 MCP count contradiction; launch docs advertise removed MCPs (alertmanager/agent-architect/incident/knowledge/runbook) as shipping
confirmedCritHigh.fixSummary: Reconcile to the authoritative 9 actually-wired MCPs (aws, azure, gcp, kubernetes, prometheus, loki, github, admin, web per mcp_manager.initialize_servers, README, CLAUDE.md, helm README). Remove alertmanager/agent-architect/incident/knowledge/runbook from all marketing/launch docs, and remove the 5 corresponding services/mcps/oap-*-mcp dirs+READMEs from the launch tree (they ship but are never wired).

controls.title: Account Management
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/services/UserAuthService.ts:494 (user create defaults is_admin: userData.isAdmin || false — least-privilege account creation); services/openagentic-api/prisma/schema.prisma:20-50 (User model with is_admin, is_locked, locked_at, groups); services/openagentic-api/src/routes/local-auth.ts:465 (POST /logout deactivates UserSession rows)
controls.gap: Account-management mechanics exist (create with non-admin default, groups, disable-on-logout) but there is NO admin-facing account disable/lock enforcement at auth time, no automated inactive-account disabling, and no last-login/usage-based account review. The User.is_locked column exists in the schema but is never read in the authentication path (see AC-7), so a 'disabled' account is not actually denied access.
controls.remediation: Wire User.is_locked enforcement into the unified auth path (see AC-7). Add an admin endpoint to enable/disable/lock accounts and an inactivity sweep. The supporting columns already exist — only enforcement is missing.

controls.title: Account Management | Automated System Account Management
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/middleware/unifiedAuth.ts:106-230 (internal-service identities authenticated via INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET timing-safe compare + X-User-Id on-behalf-of resolution); services/openagentic-api/src/auth/tokenValidator.ts:312-360 (API-key accounts via bcrypt-hashed apiKey rows with is_active + expires_at)
controls.gap: System/service accounts are managed (shared-secret + API keys with expiry), but there is no automated review/rotation of the synthetic service identity (service-<name>) and the internal-service path mints a fixed pseudo-identity rather than a managed account record.
controls.remediation: Back the internal-service identity with a real managed system-account row (auditable, rotatable) rather than a synthesized email; add API-key rotation reminders.

controls.title: Access Enforcement
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/middleware/adminGuard.ts:17-55 (admin route gate via validateAnyToken requireAdmin); services/openagentic-api/src/middleware/authorization.ts:14-50 (role gate); services/openagentic-api/src/pipeline/built-in-hooks.ts:157-180 (priority-10 PermissionService.evaluate gate on EVERY chat tool call, deny blocks dispatch); services/openagentic-api/src/routes/chat/pipeline/chat/dispatchChatToolCall.ts:347-365 (decision.approved gate); services/openagentic-api/src/routes/admin/permissions.ts:115-122 (admin-only preHandler on rule management)
controls.gap: Access enforcement is real but NOT universal. The PermissionService cascade gates only the CHAT ReAct path. The flow/workflow engine (services/shared/workflow-engine/src/nodes/mcp_tool/executor.ts on the launch branch) POSTs straight to the mcp-proxy with only an Authorization header and NO capability gate, so an agent can author a destructive tool (e.g. azure_delete_resource_group) into a flow node and execute it ungated — the documented F1 exploit. There is no single authorization choke point covering chat + flows + proxy sub-agents.
controls.remediation: Introduce one universal authorization choke point that wraps PermissionService and is crossed by chat, flow nodes, and proxy sub-agents (take the stricter of capability-matrix verdict and PermissionService verdict). Wire the flow engine executor to call it before the proxy POST.
controls.portFrom: services/openagentic-api/src/core/authz/AuthorizationSpine.ts (+ services/shared/workflow-engine/src/nodes/mcp_tool/executor.ts:136-171 ctx.authorizeNodeAction gate which the overlay already wires for the flow path)

controls.title: Information Flow Enforcement
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/services/PermissionService.ts:430-560 (classifyCallTool arg-aware parsing of call_aws/azure/gcp/kubectl: compound shell metas |,&&,;,> always gate; mutating verbs gate; read verbs auto-approve) — prevents EVAL-vs-DROP chaining on generic CLI passthrough tools
controls.gap: Flow-direction control for tool egress is enforced only on the chat path. Outbound data flow via the egress proxy (openagentic-proxy) and the flow engine is not gated by the same classifier (see AC-3). No data-egress DLP boundary on tool results returned to the model on the launch branch's flow path.
controls.remediation: Extend the arg-aware/capability classification to the egress proxy and flow nodes via the universal gate; apply the same compound-command and mutating-verb screening on all tool dispatch surfaces.

controls.title: Separation of Duties
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/services/UserAuthService.ts:494 (admin vs non-admin distinction exists); single admin role only — no maker/checker role split in code
controls.gap: Separation-of-duties is primarily an org/process control. The codebase has only a binary admin/non-admin model with no enforced duty-separation roles, but for an OSS self-hosted platform this is an organizational assignment, not a code defect.
controls.remediation: Document role assignments in the SSP; the binary admin model plus the HITL approval gate (a second human approving a model's destructive action) provides a partial technical maker/checker.

controls.title: Least Privilege
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/services/UserAuthService.ts:494 (is_admin defaults false); services/openagentic-api/src/services/PermissionService.ts:160-340 (DEFAULT_ALLOW_TOOLS = read-only globs only; destructive verbs delete/drop/truncate/destroy/terminate default to ask via DEFAULT_ASK_TOOLS:370-392 — default fall-through is ask, not allow); services/openagentic-api/src/auth/googleAuth.ts:78-84 (GOOGLE_ADMIN_EMAILS unset → zero admins, fail-closed)
controls.gap: Tool least-privilege defaults are sound on the CHAT path, but: (1) the same least-privilege gate is not applied to the flow engine (AC-3 hole); (2) PermissionService PermissionMode allows 'bypassPermissions' which short-circuits ALL checks to allow (services/openagentic-api/src/services/PermissionService.ts evaluate: mode==='bypassPermissions' → allow) — a privilege-escalation mode with no separate authorization to enter it on the launch branch; (3) the Azure path honors EXTERNAL_ADMIN_EMAILS / SKIP_GROUP_VALIDATION / KNOWN_GUEST_ADMINS env bypasses (services/openagentic-api/src/auth/azureADAuth.ts:318-335) that grant admin and skip group validation.
controls.remediation: Gate entry into bypassPermissions behind admin-only authorization and audit it; cover the flow path with the same least-privilege gate; document/constrain EXTERNAL_ADMIN_EMAILS and ensure SKIP_GROUP_VALIDATION cannot be true in a High deployment (assert at boot).
controls.portFrom: services/openagentic-api/src/core/authz/AuthorizationSpine.ts (capability-class approval matrix takes the stricter verdict, constraining broad allow-globs)

controls.title: Least Privilege | Log Use of Privileged Functions
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/middleware/unifiedAuth.ts:393-419 (API-key usage logged to adminAuditLog); services/openagentic-api/src/routes/admin/permissions.ts (writeAudit on permission_rule_added/changed); services/openagentic-api/src/services/audit/authAuditLogger.ts (auth events)
controls.gap: Privileged tool execution decisions on the chat path emit approval events, but there is no single per-decision capability audit row covering all surfaces. The overlay's AuthorizationSpine writes a logCapabilityDecision row (allow/ask/deny + approvedBy + origin) for every gated call; the launch branch lacks this unified privileged-function audit.
controls.remediation: Emit a structured per-decision audit record for every privileged/destructive tool dispatch across chat and flows.

controls.title: Unsuccessful Logon Attempts
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/routes/local-auth.ts:211-260 (login error path returns 401 with NO failed-attempt counter and NO lockout); grep for failed-attempt/lockout/locked enforcement in services/openagentic-api/src/middleware, /auth, /routes/local-auth.ts returns nothing on the launch branch; services/openagentic-api/prisma/schema.prisma:48-50 (is_locked/locked_at/locked_reason columns EXIST but are never read at auth time)
controls.gap: No account-lockout or failed-login throttle. An attacker can brute-force the local password endpoint indefinitely. The schema already carries is_locked/locked_at/locked_reason (so no migration is required), but nothing increments a failure counter, sets the lock, or denies a locked account. A coarse @fastify/rate-limit exists (config/fastify.config.ts:225) but it is a per-route request throttle, not a per-account lockout, and is not tuned for the login endpoint.
controls.remediation: Port the Redis-backed failed-login counter (rolling-window, threshold → set User.is_locked) and enforce is_locked at auth time. Columns already exist; only the counter service + enforcement middleware are needed. Ensure identical 401 shape for unknown-user vs wrong-password (no enumeration oracle).

controls.title: System Use Notification
controls.evidence: No login banner implemented; this is a deployment/UI configuration concern for an OSS self-hosted product
controls.gap: No system-use/consent banner on the login page. For an OSS codebase this is an organizational/deployment control, satisfied by the deploying agency configuring a banner.
controls.remediation: Add an optional, env-configurable consent banner string rendered on the login screen so deployers can satisfy AC-8 without forking.

controls.title: Session Termination
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/routes/local-auth.ts:121-141 (legacy verifyToken validates UserSession expires_at > now AND is_active before allowing); services/openagentic-api/src/routes/local-auth.ts:465-483 (logout sets is_active=false on all user sessions); services/openagentic-api/src/auth/googleAuth.ts:296-307 (JWT expiresIn default 86400s/24h)
controls.gap: Server-side session enforcement is NOT applied in the primary auth path. unifiedAuth.ts (used by all routes via validateAnyToken) verifies only the JWT signature+exp — it does NOT check the UserSession row's expires_at/is_active, so a logged-out (is_active=false) user's still-unexpired JWT continues to work on every route except the few that use the legacy verifyToken. Session lifetime is a fixed 24h with no idle-timeout and no admin-configurable max session age. No concurrent-session limit.
controls.remediation: Add a post-validation session-enforcement hook to unifiedAuthHook that rejects (401) when the UserSession row is expired/revoked, and (403) when User.is_locked. Make session TTL + idle timeout configurable. Columns already exist; the overlay middleware only READS them (no schema change).

controls.title: Permitted Actions Without Identification or Authentication
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/middleware/authenticate.ts:22-37 (no token → 401, DEV_AUTH_BYPASS removed v0.4.0); services/openagentic-api/src/middleware/unifiedAuth.ts:254-256 (no token from header/query/cookie → throw); services/openagentic-api/src/middleware/unifiedAuth.ts:213-238 (IP-only internal-service fallback removed; INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET timing-safe compare required) — no unauthenticated action is permitted on protected routes

controls.title: Remote Access
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/auth/tokenValidator.ts:30-40 (JWT_SECRET placeholder detection → ephemeral runtime secret with CRITICAL log); services/openagentic-api/src/middleware/unifiedAuth.ts:113-118 (internal-service auth requires shared secret, not IP); TLS/ingress termination is deployment-layer (helm/nginx)
controls.gap: All API remote access is token-authenticated, but transport encryption (TLS) is enforced at the ingress/deployment layer, not in code, and is therefore out of the launch-branch code's scope. JWT bearer tokens are also accepted via query param for SSE (unifiedAuth.ts ~line 235) and via cookies, widening the token-exposure surface for remote access.
controls.remediation: Document TLS enforcement in the SSP; constrain query-param token acceptance to SSE GET endpoints only and prefer short-lived tokens for that path.

controls.title: Access Enforcement | Role-Based Access Control
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/middleware/adminGuard.ts:64-79 (isUserAdmin checks isAdmin flag, roles[], and OpenAgentic-Admins group); services/openagentic-api/src/auth/tokenValidator.ts:210-216 (roles derived: adminFlag ? ['admin'] : []); services/openagentic-api/src/services/UserPermissionsService.ts + routes/admin/permissions.ts (admin-managed tool-permission rules)
controls.gap: RBAC is effectively binary (admin vs user) plus a separate tool-permission rule layer. There is no granular role catalog (e.g. operator/auditor/read-only-admin); roles derive solely from the isAdmin boolean. Group-based authorization exists for Azure but maps groups only to the same binary admin outcome.
controls.remediation: Introduce a named-role catalog with per-role capability bindings so least-privilege roles (auditor, operator) can be assigned without granting full admin.

controls.title: Event Logging
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/services/approval/auditAndGate.ts:88 (every tool call audited READ='auto'/MUTATING='pending'); services/openagentic-api/src/services/audit/authAuditLogger.ts:51 (login/logout/login_failed/token_refresh/sso); services/openagentic-api/src/utils/auditTrail.ts:18 (AuditEventType enum: auth, user-mgmt, config, MCP, security, credential events); services/openagentic-api/src/services/AuditLogService.ts:60 (flow governance events); credential_audit_log migration captures provider/MCP/key CRUD.
controls.gap: Event set is broad and covers the auditable-event categories High expects (logon/logoff, privileged actions, config/credential changes, tool/data access). The org-policy artifact defining the auditable-event list + review cadence is process, not code.
controls.remediation: Maintain a docs/compliance event-catalog mapping each AuditEventType + each audit source table to AU-2 event categories; keep it in sync with the activityAggregator SOURCES list.

controls.title: Content of Audit Records
controls.evidence: ToolCallAuditLog model (prisma/schema.prisma:1595) records tool_name, server_name, args, preview, classification, decision, decided_by, decided_at, user_id, session_id, message_id, origin, created_at; AuthAuditLog (schema.prisma:1305) records event, provider, success, user_id, user_email, ip_address, user_agent, detail, created_at; AdminAuditLog (schema.prisma:1275) records admin_user_id, admin_email, action, resource_type, resource_id, details, ip_address, created_at. What/when/where/who/outcome are all present.
controls.gap: Source/destination IP is captured for auth/user/admin/flow tables (ip_address columns) but tool_call_audit_log has NO ip_address column (the aggregator projects NULL::text for it — activityAggregator.ts:192). AU-3 source-of-event for tool calls is therefore weaker than for auth events.
controls.remediation: Add ip_address (and optionally user_agent) to ToolCallAuditLog + populate from request in runAuditAndGate; surface it in the aggregator tool-call projection instead of NULL.

controls.title: Audit Log Storage Capacity
controls.evidence: Audit rows persist to Postgres (prisma); capacity is an operational/sizing concern of the deployment (PVC sizing in downstream helm values), not the OSS code.
controls.gap: No in-code storage-capacity monitoring, but this is an org/deployment-sizing control for an OSS codebase.
controls.remediation: Document recommended Postgres volume sizing + alerting in the deployment guide; optionally emit a Prometheus gauge for audit table row counts.

controls.title: Response to Audit Logging Process Failures
controls.evidence: auditAndGate.ts:133 — a MUTATING tool call whose pending-audit INSERT fails returns allowed:false (FAIL-SAFE: the mutation is blocked when it cannot be audited). This is a real, correct AU-5(1)/(2)-style failure response for the highest-risk path.
controls.gap: The fail-safe is partial and inconsistent: (1) READ tool audits, auth events (authAuditLogger.ts:62), admin actions (AuditLogger.ts), user-query audits (AuditLogger.ts logUserQuery) and flow-sink dispatch (AuditLogService.ts:85) all SWALLOW write failures and continue — so most audit-process failures are silent, with no alert/shutdown. No real-time alert (AU-5(1)) or system-shutdown/overwrite option on audit failure exists. (2) No detection or operator notification when the audit store is unavailable.
controls.remediation: Emit a metric/alert (Prometheus counter + loggers.error at WARN→ERROR) on every audit-write failure; add a configurable AU-5 policy (alert vs. block) for non-mutating paths; consider a circuit-breaker that fails non-essential requests when the audit store is down for a configurable window.

controls.title: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting
controls.evidence: Unified review feed implemented: services/openagentic-api/src/routes/admin-audit-logs.ts (GET /api/admin/audit-logs + /stats + /errors + /sessions + /export) mounted at server.ts:1132; backed by services/openagentic-api/src/services/audit/activityAggregator.ts (UNION ALL across ~13 tables, filter by type/actor/resource/time/outcome, stats by type+outcome, CSV export). Admin-guarded per-route via adminMiddleware.
controls.gap: Review/analysis is on-demand admin UI only. No automated correlation/anomaly analysis (AU-6(3) org-wide correlation, AU-6(5) integrate w/ vuln/perf data) and no scheduled review tooling. The /errors endpoint surfaces failures but there is no alerting on review findings.
controls.remediation: Document the admin Audit Logs page + export as the review mechanism; for AU-6(3)/(4) integration, lean on the AuditLogStreamingService SIEM offload (see AU-6 evidence + AU-9 below) once extended to all tables, and document the SIEM as the correlation/analysis system.

controls.title: Audit Record Reduction and Report Generation
controls.evidence: activityAggregator.queryActivity supports on-demand reduction by type/actor/resource/time-window/outcome with correct cross-source pagination (activityAggregator.ts:638) WITHOUT altering source records (read-only $queryRawUnsafe SELECTs); admin-audit-logs.ts streamCsv produces report output (CSV export endpoint). Reduction preserves the original records (separate read path).
controls.gap: Reduction/reporting is limited to the ~13 tables wired into the aggregator; tables not in SOURCES (e.g. raw chat messages) are out of scope of the report tool. Acceptable for AU-7 base.
controls.remediation: None required for AU-7 base; keep SOURCES in sync as new audit tables are added.

controls.title: Time Stamps
controls.evidence: Audit timestamps default to DB time via @default(now()) on every audit model (e.g. ToolCallAuditLog.created_at schema.prisma; AuthAuditLog.created_at schema.prisma:1316), giving a single authoritative Postgres clock; created_at is stored as timestamptz (UTC).
controls.gap: Some writers pass an app-side timestamp instead of the DB default (auditTrail.ts:166 created_at: event.timestamp || new Date(); AuditLogger.logAdminAction uses new Date()), so records can carry the API container clock rather than the DB clock. There is no in-code assertion that the host/container is NTP-synced to an authoritative source (AU-8(1)) — that is an infrastructure control.
controls.remediation: Prefer DB-generated now() for all audit inserts (drop app-supplied created_at); document NTP/chrony requirement on the DB + API hosts in the deployment guide to satisfy AU-8(1).

controls.title: Protection of Audit Information
controls.evidence: App-layer append-only is real and tested: services/openagentic-api/src/services/approval/auditLog.ts exports only insertAuditRow + a single guarded decideAuditRow (updateMany WHERE decision='pending', auditLog.ts:58-62) with NO delete path; pinned by services/openagentic-api/src/__tests__/architecture/audit-log-append-only.source-regression.test.ts (no delete/deleteMany, only the guarded update). A DB-level immutability trigger EXISTS in prisma/migrations/20260218_audit_immutable/migration.sql (BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE → RAISE EXCEPTION). All audit routes are admin-guarded (adminMiddleware).
controls.gap: CRITICAL: the DB-level AU-9 immutability trigger is NEVER APPLIED at runtime. The deploy contract is `prisma db push --skip-generate` (docker-entrypoint.sh:146), which syncs schema.prisma but does NOT execute SQL migration files — pinned by docker-entrypoint-runs-migrations.source-regression.test.ts (deliberately removed `migrate deploy`). So prevent_audit_modification triggers are dead code; protection rests ONLY on app-layer source discipline, which does not stop a DBA, SQL-injection, or any out-of-band UPDATE/DELETE. Second gap: even the written trigger covers only admin_audit_log, user_query_audit, credential_exchange_audit, synth_capability_audit — it does NOT cover tool_call_audit_log, auth_audit_log, flow_audit_log, credential_audit_log, or dlp_findings. Third: tamper-evidence is fragmented — AuditLogger.ts writes a real chain_hash/previous_hash chain (AuditLogger.ts:297) BUT the previous-hash comes from an in-memory cache (lastAdminAuditHash, AuditLogger.ts:76,327) with no advisory lock, so concurrent writers race and fork the chain; auditTrail.ts:143 writes plain rows with only a self-recomputable per-row checksum in details (detects nothing); and unifiedAuth.ts:398 + permissions/v3-extras write admin_audit_log with NO hash at all — so the chain has unhashed gaps.
controls.remediation: 1) Apply the immutability triggers at runtime: either switch the entrypoint to `prisma migrate deploy` for migrations, or move the trigger SQL into scripts/postgres-init (alongside 01-extensions.sql) so it runs on DB init regardless of db push. 2) Extend prevent_audit_modification triggers to tool_call_audit_log (with a narrow exception allowing only pending→terminal transition, or move the decision to a separate decisions table so the audit row stays truly immutable), auth_audit_log, flow_audit_log, credential_audit_log, dlp_findings. 3) Make chain-hashing the single path for ALL admin_audit_log writers (route unifiedAuth/permissions/v3-extras/auditTrail through AuditLogger.logAdminAction) and replace the in-memory previous-hash cache with a pg_advisory_lock (xact)-guarded read-latest+insert so the chain cannot fork under concurrency. 4) Run a least-privilege DB role for the API that lacks UPDATE/DELETE on audit tables.

controls.title: Protection of Audit Information | Store on Separate Physical Systems
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/services/AuditLogStreamingService.ts dispatches audit rows to an external sink (AUDIT_LOG_SINK=stdout|datadog|splunk|fluentd|s3) with batching — an off-box copy mechanism that satisfies the intent of AU-9(2).
controls.gap: Only FlowAuditLog rows are streamed off-box (AuditLogService.ts:85 sink.dispatch). The other ~12 audit sources (tool calls, auth, admin actions, user queries, DLP, credential, agent, webhook, workflow) are NOT forwarded — so the bulk of audit data has no separate-system copy.
controls.remediation: Wire AuditLogStreamingService.dispatch into every audit writer (or add a single post-insert fan-out), so all audit tables stream to the configured SIEM/object store; document the off-box backup target as the AU-9(2)/AU-11 system of record.

controls.title: Protection of Audit Information | Cryptographic Protection
controls.evidence: AuditLogger.computeChainHash (AuditLogger.ts:90) computes SHA-256(previousHash + eventType + userId + action + timestamp + details) and stores chain_hash/previous_hash on admin_audit_log; verifyAdminAuditChain (AuditLogger.ts:130) re-derives and reports the first broken link (@@index([chain_hash]) in schema).
controls.gap: Cryptographic integrity is (a) admin_audit_log only — not applied to tool_call/auth/flow/user-query audit tables; (b) defeated by the in-memory previous-hash cache race (forks under concurrency, see AU-9); (c) not a keyed/signed MAC — a tamperer with write access can recompute a consistent chain (no HMAC secret / no external notarization); (d) verifyAdminAuditChain is not exposed via any admin route, so the integrity check cannot be run operationally.
controls.remediation: Use an HMAC with a secret (or sign with the existing Ed25519 license key infra) rather than a bare SHA-256 chain; persist the previous-hash read under an advisory lock; extend chaining/signing to the other audit tables or periodically notarize the SIEM stream; expose verifyAdminAuditChain via an admin endpoint + a scheduled integrity job.

controls.title: Audit Record Retention
controls.evidence: No audit-retention mechanism found on the launch branch: grep for retention/purge over audit tables (BackgroundJobService.ts and elsewhere) yields no audit-table retention job; there is no AUDIT_RETENTION_DAYS env, no scheduled purge, and no documented retention period. Audit rows accumulate indefinitely (which preserves data but does not implement a defined retention policy).
controls.gap: FedRAMP High requires a defined retention period (commonly 90 days online + 1 year archived) enforced and documented. Neither enforcement nor a stated policy exists in code.
controls.remediation: Add a configurable retention policy (e.g. AUDIT_ONLINE_RETENTION_DAYS) implemented as a BackgroundJobService task that archives audit rows to the AU-9(2) off-box sink before deleting online rows older than the window — and ensure any such delete is the ONLY sanctioned exception to the immutability trigger (run as a privileged maintenance role). Document the retention period in the compliance docs.

controls.title: Audit Record Generation
controls.evidence: Tool-call generation is wired at the two live convergence seams: dispatchTool.ts and buildChatV2Deps.ts both call runAuditAndGate/auditMcpExecutionSeam (auditAndGate.ts:88,245), with a single-pass AUDIT_DONE_FLAG so every chat AND subagent tool call produces exactly one row (origin 'chat'|'subagent'). Auth generation wired into routes/local-auth.ts (login:345, logout:484, login_failed:244,261) and routes/auth.ts (Azure:2056,2077) via logAuthEvent (authAuditLogger.ts:51). Admin-action generation via adminAuditLog.create in unifiedAuth.ts:398 (API-key request logging) + permissions/v3-extras routes + AuditLogger.logAdminAction (AuditLogger.ts:284). Records are admin-selectable/queryable via the unified feed (AU-12(1)-style system-wide trail across components).
controls.gap: Admin-action coverage is partial/inconsistent — only some admin routes call an audit write, and there is no single enforced middleware that audits EVERY privileged mutation, so coverage depends on each route author remembering to log. (Not a generation-capability gap, a completeness gap.)
controls.remediation: Introduce a mandatory admin-mutation audit hook (in adminMiddleware or a Fastify onResponse for non-GET /api/admin/* routes) so every privileged action is generated through one path that also chain-hashes (ties to AU-9(3)).

controls.title: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/routes/local-auth.ts:214-313 (local username/password login: bcrypt verify + JWT issue + server-side UserSession upsert); services/openagentic-api/src/auth/tokenValidator.ts:866-1110 (single SoT validateAnyToken routes local JWT / Azure AD / Google / API key by payload claims)

controls.title: MFA to Privileged Accounts / network access
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/services/AzureTokenService.ts:30-82,406-465 (MFA-freshness window on cached OBO tokens: decodes auth_time/iat, forces refresh when MFA is stale beyond AZURE_OBO_MFA_FRESHNESS_MINUTES, default 30m)
controls.gap: MFA is enforced only by the upstream IdP (Azure AD Conditional Access / Google), with the platform honoring auth_time freshness for cloud OBO. For LOCAL accounts (the default OSS install path — admin@openagentic.local) there is NO MFA whatsoever: local-auth.ts:214 authenticates on password alone. A High-baseline ATO requires MFA for all privileged access; the local provider cannot satisfy IA-2(1) without an external IdP.
controls.remediation: Document that FedRAMP-High deployments MUST set AUTH_PROVIDER=azure-ad|google and disable local password login for privileged users; OR add TOTP/WebAuthn enrollment to the local provider. At minimum gate is_admin local accounts behind an MFA factor before claiming IA-2(1).

controls.title: Replay-Resistant Authentication
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/auth/azureADAuth.ts:131-197 (Azure AD tokens verified against tenant JWKS with RS256, audience=clientId, issuer pinned per validIssuers); services/openagentic-api/src/auth/googleAuth.ts:186-191 (Google ID tokens verified via google-auth-library verifyIdToken with audience=clientId). Signed, short-lived, audience-bound assertions are replay-resistant.

controls.title: Device / Service-to-Service Identification and Authentication
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/middleware/unifiedAuth.ts:103-205 (API side: x-request-from internal callers REQUIRE INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET compared with crypto.timingSafeEqual; IP-only fallback explicitly removed in v0.4.0; blocks on mismatch/unset). services/openagentic-api/src/services/llm-providers/util/mintInterServiceSystemToken.ts:30-34 (api mints oa_sys_ tokens as HMAC_SHA256(INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET,'openagentic-system-token')).
controls.gap: The mcp-proxy VERIFIER does not enforce the HMAC the api MINTS. services/openagentic-mcp-proxy/src/main.py:668-680 grants full system-root is_admin=True on a PREFIX match of 'oa_sys_' alone — it never reads INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET, never recomputes the HMAC, never constant-time compares. Any caller sending Authorization: Bearer oa_sys_anything gets cluster-admin tool access. The contract is documented and a RED test exists (services/openagentic-mcp-proxy/tests/test_jwt_auth.py:17-28: 'oa_sys_<bad-suffix> -> 401 (currently passes - fix this)', must use hmac.compare_digest), but main.py does not satisfy it (no bootstrap_jwt_keys / compare_digest / _expected_system_suffix symbols present).
controls.remediation: In services/openagentic-mcp-proxy/src/main.py get_user_info, replace the prefix-only oa_sys_ branch with: require INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET to be set (else 401), compute expected = base64url(HMAC_SHA256(secret,'openagentic-system-token')), and accept only if hmac.compare_digest(provided_suffix, expected); reject empty/legacy-plain-secret suffixes with 401 invalid_system_token. This makes test_jwt_auth.py green. NOT portable from overlay — the working-tree main.py:668-680 has the identical prefix-only bug, so this must be built, not ported.

controls.title: Authenticator Management
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/routes/admin-api-tokens.ts:175-204 (API keys minted oa_<base64url(32B)>, stored as bcrypt hash key_hash, with expires_at + is_active revoke); services/openagentic-api/src/routes/local-auth.ts:82 (bcrypt password hashing); services/openagentic-api/src/services/vault.service.ts:391-427 (credential at-rest crypto, AES-256-GCM authenticated encryption for new 'local2:' writes, legacy CBC read-only); prisma/migrations/20260218_credential_expiry + 20260218_credential_audit_log (credential expiry tracking + audit).
controls.gap: (1) No server-side password-complexity / minimum-length policy: a grep of local-auth.ts / AdminValidationService finds no length/complexity check (IA-5(1)(a)). (2) bcrypt cost factor hardcoded at 10 (local-auth.ts:82, admin-api-tokens.ts:190) — acceptable but should be tunable for High. (3) vault.service.ts:60 falls back to crypto.randomBytes(32) when LOCAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY is unset, making all stored credentials unrecoverable after restart (availability) and silently disabling a managed key (no fail-closed).
controls.remediation: Add a password-policy validator (min 12 chars, complexity, dictionary/known-breached check) enforced in the local create/change-password handlers; raise/parameterize bcrypt cost via BCRYPT_ROUNDS (>=12); fail-closed at boot if LOCAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY is unset in production rather than generating an ephemeral key.

controls.title: Password-Based Authentication
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/routes/local-auth.ts:228-313 — login validates only presence of username/password then bcrypt-compares; no complexity, minimum-length, reuse, or breached-password enforcement anywhere in the local provider. force_password_change exists (local-auth.ts:276-294) but no policy on the NEW password.
controls.gap: FedRAMP-High IA-5(1) requires enforced password composition/length, prohibition of known-compromised passwords, and reuse restrictions for local authenticators. None is implemented; any password (including trivial) is accepted on create/change.
controls.remediation: Implement a shared validatePasswordPolicy() (length >= 12-15, complexity or NIST 800-63B length-based, screen against a breached-password list e.g. HIBP k-anonymity or a local denylist, optional reuse history table) and call it in the password create/change/reset handlers (local-auth.ts change-password + auth.ts reset paths). Reject non-compliant passwords with 400.

controls.title: Public Key-Based Authentication / Signed Assertions
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/auth/azureADAuth.ts:131-197 (jwks-rsa client fetches Azure AD signing keys by kid, jwt.verify enforces RS256 signature + audience + issuer); services/openagentic-api/src/auth/googleAuth.ts:186-191 (verifyIdToken validates Google's signing keys + audience). External-IdP authenticators are validated by signature against trusted JWKS, not by shared secret.

controls.title: Authentication Feedback
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/routes/local-auth.ts:255,272 (failed login returns generic 'Invalid credentials' for both unknown-user and wrong-password — no user-enumeration oracle; the absence of distinguishing responses is the IA-6/IA-5 obfuscation behavior). Password material is bcrypt-only and never echoed.

controls.title: Identification and Authentication (Non-Organizational / Federated Users)
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/auth/tokenValidator.ts:824-830,942-1103 (AUTH_PROVIDER selects azure-ad / google / hybrid; federated tokens validated through the respective verified-assertion path); services/openagentic-api/src/auth/googleAuth.ts:78-106,248-258 (GOOGLE_ADMIN_EMAILS / allowedDomains hosted-domain (hd) gating for federated identities).

controls.title: Service Identification and Authentication
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/middleware/unifiedAuth.ts:113-205 (api authenticates internal services by INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET with timingSafeEqual, and treats X-User-Id only as an identity claim AFTER the secret authenticates the caller — clean separation of service-auth vs acted-on-behalf-of identity).
controls.gap: Same root cause as IA-3: the mcp-proxy does NOT authenticate the api as a service. main.py:668-680 trusts the oa_sys_ prefix unconditionally, and main.py:649-661 grants is_admin=True for a NO-credentials request (test_auth_hardening.py invariant #2 explicitly forbids the no-creds branch from granting is_admin). A network peer that reaches the proxy with no Authorization header obtains system-admin context.
controls.remediation: (1) Remove the no-credentials -> system-admin branch in get_user_info (main.py:649-661); return 401 missing_authorization. (2) Verify oa_sys_ via HMAC as in IA-3. (3) Add bootstrap_jwt_keys() fail-closed boot check (rejects unset/dev-secret signing keys) per test_auth_hardening.py. Build-not-port: overlay main.py has the identical defects.

controls.title: Unsuccessful Logon Attempts (account lockout)
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/routes/local-auth.ts:243-273 (on failed login the launch branch logs an auth-audit event via logAuthEvent but does NOT increment any failed-attempt counter nor lock the account). The User model HAS is_locked/locked_at/locked_reason columns (prisma/schema.prisma:48-50) but on the launch branch they are set ONLY by the chat scope-violation ladder (services/openagentic-api/src/services/ScopeEnforcementService.ts:204-268) and read on Azure login (routes/auth.ts:1888) — there is NO brute-force / failed-password lockout. middleware/sessionEnforcement.ts does not exist on the launch branch (confirmed: 'exists on disk but not in oss-launch/a3-fedramp').
controls.gap: No automatic lockout after N consecutive failed authentication attempts within a window — a core High-baseline brute-force control. Local login can be brute-forced indefinitely (the global @fastify/rate-limit is per-IP request throttling, not per-account failed-auth lockout).
controls.portFrom: services/openagentic-api/src/services/UserAuthService.ts (overlay lines 590-720: incrementFailedAttempts/clearFailedAttempts/lockAccount, Redis-backed rolling-window counter keyed auth:failed-login:<email>, MAX_FAILED_LOGIN_ATTEMPTS=5 / FAILED_LOGIN_WINDOW_SECONDS=900, sets is_locked/locked_at/locked_reason + CRITICAL audit) AND services/openagentic-api/src/middleware/sessionEnforcement.ts (overlay: enforceSession/sessionEnforcementHook returns 403 ACCOUNT_LOCKED, wired in services/openagentic-api/src/middleware/unifiedAuth.ts overlay lines 14,597,613)
controls.remediation: Port the overlay's Redis-backed failed-login counter (UserAuthService.incrementFailedAttempts/lockAccount) and call it from the local-auth.ts 401 path (and clearFailedAttempts on success); port sessionEnforcement.ts and wire sessionEnforcementHook into unifiedAuth so a locked User row returns 403 ACCOUNT_LOCKED on the next request. Both are already-written, OSS-clean, no enterprise content — surgical port. Counter is fail-open on Redis outage (won't turn 401 into 500) and avoids a user-enumeration oracle by design.

controls.title: Session Termination
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/routes/local-auth.ts:111-155,319-338 (server-side UserSession with expires_at/is_active; verifyToken (used by the local-auth router) re-checks the session is active + unexpired and updates last_accessed_at — a revoked/expired session is rejected even with a valid JWT, for routes using that guard).
controls.gap: The general request path (unifiedAuth + validateAnyToken) validates the JWT signature/expiry but does NOT re-check UserSession.is_active/expires_at — only the local-auth router's own verifyToken does. A token revoked server-side (is_active=false) before its 24h JWT exp still authenticates on every other route. The overlay closes this with sessionEnforcement.ts (AC-12 SESSION_REVOKED/SESSION_EXPIRED 401) wired into unifiedAuth; the launch branch lacks that middleware (file absent on branch).
controls.portFrom: services/openagentic-api/src/middleware/sessionEnforcement.ts (overlay enforceSession: 401 SESSION_REVOKED when !is_active, 401 SESSION_EXPIRED when expires_at past; sessionEnforcementHook wired in unifiedAuth.ts overlay:597,613)
controls.remediation: Port sessionEnforcement.ts and invoke sessionEnforcementHook in the unifiedAuth auth/admin hooks so server-side session revocation/expiry is enforced platform-wide, not just on the local-auth router (closes the revoked-token-still-valid gap).

controls.title: No Embedded Unencrypted Static Authenticators / Credential At-Rest Protection
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/services/vault.service.ts:391-427 (LLM provider credentials + OBO tokens encrypted with AES-256-GCM, authenticated, 'local2:' format, with legacy CBC read-only for migration); services/openagentic-api/src/services/llm-providers/CredentialEncryptionService.ts:4-67 (sensitive auth_config fields encrypted before persistence); .githooks/pre-commit blocks committed secret patterns (per CLAUDE.md). API keys are bcrypt-hashed at rest (admin-api-tokens.ts:190).

controls.title: Identifier Management
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/auth/tokenValidator.ts:964 (Google identities namespaced 'google_<sub>'); UserContext.tenantId set per provider ('local'/'api-key'/tid) at tokenValidator.ts:1021,1164; services/openagentic-api/src/services/UserAuthService.ts:461-509 (Azure identities keyed on oid). Identifiers are provider-scoped and not reused across realms.

controls.title: Re-authentication
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/routes/local-auth.ts:312 (JWT expiresIn 24h forces periodic re-authentication); services/openagentic-api/src/services/AzureTokenService.ts:439-465 (MFA-freshness forces OBO re-auth when auth_time exceeds the freshness window — a re-authentication trigger for elevated cloud access).
controls.gap: No explicit re-authentication on privilege escalation or for sensitive admin actions beyond the 24h token lifetime; local sessions do not require step-up re-auth for admin operations.
controls.remediation: Add a step-up re-authentication requirement (recent-auth-time check) before high-impact admin/credential operations; consider shorter session lifetime for is_admin sessions.

controls.title: Boundary Protection
controls.evidence: services/mcps/oap-web-mcp/ssrf_guard.py:67 (deny_if_private) + server.py:185-226 (fetch_with_ssrf_guard pre-flight + per-hop); services/openagentic-api/src/services/K8sNetworkPolicyService.ts:1 (admin-managed NetworkPolicies)
controls.gap: Application-edge egress SSRF guard is strong for the web MCP, but there is NO network-segmentation enforced by the deployment artifacts: the Helm chart ships zero NetworkPolicy templates (grep 'kind: NetworkPolicy' over helm/openagentic/templates/* returns nothing), so by default every pod (api, mcp-proxy, workflows, postgres, ollama) can reach every other pod and arbitrary egress. K8sNetworkPolicyService only *reads/manages* policies reactively from the admin Network-Security panel; it does not author a default-deny baseline. The mcp-proxy spawns built-in MCP servers as subprocesses (main.py:302) with no per-MCP network confinement. A managed boundary (managed interfaces / deny-by-default) is therefore not substantiated by code.
controls.remediation: Ship default-deny + scoped-allow NetworkPolicy templates in helm/openagentic/templates for each component (api↔postgres/redis/milvus/mcp-proxy, ui↔api, mcp-proxy↔upstream MCP endpoints only), gated behind a values flag, mirroring the overlay's synth-executor policy. Document the managed interfaces.

controls.title: Boundary Protection | Prevent Exfiltration (DLP)
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/services/DLPScannerService.ts:1-100 (50+ rules across credential/pii/infrastructure/compliance/injection, severity→action); services/openagentic-api/src/routes/chat/pipeline/dlp-scan.stage.ts:21-70 (DlpScanStage priority 25 scans user_input pre-LLM, blocks/redacts + emits dlp_blocked); services/shared/workflow-engine/src/secretRedaction.ts:38-50 (redactString replaces resolved secrets with [redacted:<name>]) used in WorkflowExecutionEngine.ts

controls.title: Boundary Protection | External Telecommunications Services / Outbound SSRF
controls.evidence: services/mcps/oap-web-mcp/ssrf_guard.py:67-127 rejects RFC1918/loopback/link-local(IMDS)/reserved/multicast IPv4+IPv6, *.svc.cluster.local, and literal IMDS hosts (169.254.169.254/metadata.google.internal/metadata.azure.com) before DNS; server.py:201-226 re-validates every redirect hop (follow_redirects=False) capping at max_redirects to defeat 302→IMDS rebinding

controls.title: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity
controls.evidence: services/mcps/oap-web-mcp/server.py:129-130 (SSL_CONTEXT = ssl.create_default_context()) + 205-208 (httpx.AsyncClient(verify=SSL_CONTEXT)); services/openagentic-api/src/server.ts:833 ('Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains'); helm/openagentic/templates/ingress.yaml:16-20 (TLS secret block)
controls.gap: TLS-in-transit is enforced on the public edge and on user-facing web fetches, but several internal/outbound paths disable or omit verification: services/openagentic-api/src/services/DataSourceService.ts:249 and :420 set ssl:{rejectUnauthorized:false} for user-configured Postgres data sources (MITM-able); services/shared/workflow-engine/src/nodes/splunk_search/executor.ts:58 uses https.Agent({rejectUnauthorized:false}); services/shared/workflow-engine/src/nodes/outlook_email/executor.ts:47 sets secure:false. The SearXNG search path (server.py:283) builds httpx.AsyncClient WITHOUT verify=SSL_CONTEXT. Ingress TLS is opt-in (conditional on .Values.ingress.tlsSecret) rather than required, and there is no enforced service-to-service mTLS — internal hops (api↔mcp-proxy↔workflows↔postgres↔redis) run plaintext HTTP by default.
controls.remediation: Make TLS verification the non-overridable default: remove rejectUnauthorized:false (or gate it behind an explicit, audited 'insecure-dev-only' flag that is rejected when a FedRAMP/prod profile is set); pass verify=SSL_CONTEXT to the SearXNG client; require ingress.tlsSecret (fail template render without it in the high profile); add a mesh/mTLS option or document that internal traffic must run inside an mTLS service mesh for the ATO boundary.

controls.title: Transmission Confidentiality | Cryptographic Protection
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/server.ts:833 HSTS; web MCP uses system default-context TLS (server.py:205-208)
controls.gap: Same as SC-8: the cryptographic protection of internal transmissions is not guaranteed (plaintext internal HTTP, TLS-verify disabled on data-source/splunk/outlook paths). No evidence of a FIPS-validated TLS module or a minimum TLS-version/cipher floor being pinned anywhere in code or chart.
controls.remediation: Pin TLS >=1.2 with a FedRAMP-approved cipher suite at the ingress and on outbound httpx/https.Agent clients; assert FIPS mode (Node --force-fips / OpenSSL FIPS provider) in the high-baseline build; remove the rejectUnauthorized:false paths.

controls.title: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/services/vault.service.ts:59-61 (LOCAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY from env, else crypto.randomBytes(32)); :138-140 token encryption; HashiCorp Vault AppRole auth :84-118 with token renewal :108-130; services/openagentic-api/src/utils/internalKeyReader.ts:1-60 (projected-Secret hot-reload for internal key rotation)
controls.gap: Two real key-management weaknesses: (1) vault.service.ts:60 falls back to an EPHEMERAL crypto.randomBytes(32) key when LOCAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY is unset — every restart generates a new key, silently rendering all previously-encrypted at-rest credentials undecryptable, and there is no startup guard that refuses to boot in a prod/high profile without a stable key. (2) No key-rotation/re-wrap procedure or key-derivation (raw hex key used directly as the AES key; no KDF, no per-tenant DEK/KEK split). Vault Transit is used opportunistically (:325-340) but the code always falls back to the local key, so a single LOCAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY is the effective root of trust for at-rest secrets.
controls.remediation: Refuse to start (process.exit) when LOCAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY is unset/short and a high/prod profile is active rather than minting a throwaway key; document and implement a key-rotation re-encrypt path (encrypt-existing-credentials.ts exists as a re-wrap seam); prefer Vault Transit as the canonical KEK with local only as a clearly-labeled dev fallback.

controls.title: Cryptographic Protection (FIPS-validated)
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/services/vault.service.ts:393-399 (encryptLocal uses AES-256-GCM, 12-byte random IV, getAuthTag); :407-427 decrypts local2: GCM + legacy local: CBC; services/openagentic-api/src/services/llm-providers/CredentialEncryptionService.ts:46-63 (encrypts apiKey/clientSecret/token fields); services/openagentic-api/src/middleware/unifiedAuth.ts:113-114 (crypto.timingSafeEqual for internal secret)
controls.gap: The algorithms chosen are FIPS-approved (AES-256-GCM, SHA-family), and authenticated encryption + constant-time comparison are correct. BUT SC-13 at the High baseline requires FIPS 140-2/3 *validated* modules: there is no evidence the runtime forces Node/OpenSSL into FIPS mode, and the legacy AES-256-CBC decrypt path (vault.service.ts:427, unauthenticated) is still accepted on read. Whether the deployed crypto is a validated module is unsubstantiated by code.
controls.remediation: Enable and assert OpenSSL FIPS provider / Node FIPS at boot for the high profile and add a startup self-test; complete migration off CBC and remove the local: read path once data is re-wrapped to GCM (local2:).

controls.title: Protection of Information at Rest
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/services/llm-providers/CredentialEncryptionService.ts:13-22 (SENSITIVE_FIELDS) + :42-63 encryptAuthConfig wraps provider secrets as local2: before DB write; vault.service.ts:393-399 AES-256-GCM; services/openagentic-api/prisma/migrations/20260218_credential_audit_log + 20260218_credential_expiry (credential lifecycle); services/openagentic-api/src/scripts/encrypt-existing-credentials.ts (backfill)
controls.gap: Field-level encryption of credentials at rest is implemented and authenticated; depends on the SC-12 stable-key fix to be durable. Database/volume-level encryption is an infra/process control (NA in code).

controls.title: Mobile Code / Browser Content Security Policy
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/server.ts:835 sets Content-Security-Policy default-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none'; plus :832 X-Frame-Options DENY, :836 Permissions-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff
controls.gap: The CSP weakens script-src and style-src with 'unsafe-inline' AND script-src 'unsafe-eval', which substantially defeats the XSS-mitigation intent of a CSP (any injected inline script executes). connect-src/img-src also allow broad https:. For an ATO this is a recognized SC-18 finding.
controls.remediation: Move the UI to nonce/hash-based script-src and drop 'unsafe-inline'/'unsafe-eval' (Vite supports nonces); tighten connect-src to the known API/WSS origins. If pyodide/in-browser sandbox needs wasm-eval, scope 'wasm-unsafe-eval' rather than full 'unsafe-eval'.

controls.title: Session Authenticity / Inter-Service Trust
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/middleware/unifiedAuth.ts:113-114 (crypto.timingSafeEqual on the internal service secret, length-checked first); services/openagentic-api/src/utils/internalKeyReader.ts (single SoT internal key from projected Secret with mtime hot-reload for rotation); services/openagentic-proxy/src/middleware/auth.ts:28-31 (service-to-service Bearer + X-Agent-Proxy marker, else delegates token validation to the API /api/auth/validate-token)
controls.gap: Inter-service trust uses a shared symmetric internal key with constant-time comparison and rotation support — adequate. Hardening opportunity (not a gap): the openagentic-proxy internal-key path (auth.ts:30) uses a plain === string compare for the Bearer value rather than timingSafeEqual; could be tightened, and the trust is bearer-token not mTLS (see SC-8).

controls.title: Process Isolation (Sandboxing of Untrusted Code)
controls.evidence: services/shared/workflow-engine/src/sandbox.ts:1-30 + runSandboxed (isolated-vm V8 isolate, no process/require/globalThis, timeoutMs + memoryCapMb hard caps) used by code/condition/transform nodes; services/shared/workflow-engine/src/nodes/k8s_sandbox_run/executor.ts:30-41 (ephemeral namespace + ResourceQuota + NetworkPolicy + teardown)
controls.gap: The two server-side untrusted-code paths that ship on the launch branch are reasonably isolated (isolated-vm; k8s ephemeral namespace). The stronger sandbox — the synth-executor (Python tool synthesis runner referenced in CLAUDE.md) with AST validation, locked __builtins__, blocked subprocess/socket/importlib, setrlimit (AS/CPU/NOFILE/NPROC/FSIZE), seccompProfile, readOnlyRootFilesystem, runAsNonRoot, dropped capabilities and a default-deny NetworkPolicy — is NOT present on the launch branch (services/synth-executor exists only in the upstream overlay). If/when synth tool-synthesis is enabled, the launch branch has no hardened executor and CLAUDE.md still advertises it.
controls.remediation: Either keep synth-executor excluded from the launch surface (and remove the CLAUDE.md/docs references so the advertised-but-absent feature isn't an audit discrepancy), or port the hardened executor + its k8s securityContext/NetworkPolicy as the canonical sandbox.

controls.title: Denial of Service Protection
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/server.ts:820-826 (comment: per-route rateLimiter middleware via rateLimiter.ts; global @fastify/rate-limit intentionally avoided); ssrf_guard redirect cap (server.py:226 too_many_redirects); sandbox.ts memory/CPU caps
controls.gap: Rate limiting is per-route (opt-in) rather than a global default, so any route that forgets to attach rateLimiter is unthrottled; there is no documented connection/request-size DoS floor at the app layer beyond the nginx proxy-body-size annotation (ingress.yaml:9, 50m). Resource-exhaustion protection for the LLM/agent loop (turn caps) exists elsewhere but a uniform DoS posture is not substantiated.
controls.remediation: Apply a default rate-limit at the framework level (encapsulated so health/metrics are exempt) and document the per-route overrides; add request-size and concurrency caps.

controls.title: Information in Shared Resources
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/services/llm-providers/CredentialEncryptionService.ts:78-99 (decryptAuthConfig DELETES fields that fail to decrypt so ciphertext is never forwarded as a credential); secretRedaction.ts:38-50 strips resolved secrets from persisted/emitted workflow data; dlp-scan.stage.ts redacts before persistence/embedding/LLM
controls.gap: Cross-context secret leakage is actively prevented (redaction before persistence/emit, drop-on-decrypt-failure). Adequate for the code-substantiable portion.

controls.title: Separation of System and User Functionality
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/server.ts:849-851 (rlsContextHook preHandler sets Row-Level-Security context per authenticated request — FedRAMP AC-4 note); unifiedAuth tokenType classification (local/azure-ad/api-key/internal) at unifiedAuth.ts:78-392; adminMiddleware admin-only protection (server.ts:818)
controls.gap: Admin vs user vs internal-system surfaces are separated by middleware and DB RLS; substantiated.

controls.title: Public Key Infrastructure Certificates
controls.evidence: No code-level PKI/CA issuance; ingress TLS secret (ingress.yaml:16-20) is supplied by the operator (cert-manager/external), an organizational/deployment process control rather than an application control.

controls.title: Flaw Remediation
controls.evidence: .github/workflows/oss-integrity.yml (only runs tools/verify-oss-integrity.sh); .github/workflows/sonar.yml (SonarQube SAST only); no dependabot.yml / npm-audit / trivy / grype / osv-scanner anywhere in the launch tree
controls.gap: No automated dependency or container vulnerability scanning. CI runs an OSS-sanity shell check + SonarQube SAST, but nothing flags known-CVE dependencies in pnpm-lock.yaml / Python requirements / Docker base images. SI-2 for High requires identifying, reporting, and time-bounded remediation of flaws — the repo has SECURITY.md (48h ack, 5-day response) for externally reported flaws but no proactive scanning to discover them.
controls.remediation: Add a .github/dependabot.yml (npm/pip/docker/github-actions ecosystems) plus a CI job running `pnpm audit --audit-level=high` (fail on high/critical) and a container image scan (trivy/grype) for each service Dockerfile. Gate PRs to main on it. Track remediation SLAs in SECURITY.md.

controls.title: Malicious Code Protection
controls.evidence: .githooks/pre-commit (blocks AWS keys + secret patterns from commits); services/openagentic-api/src/services/DLPScannerService.ts + routes/chat/pipeline/dlp-scan.stage.ts (scans/blocks credentials+PII at the chat edge, priority 25, fail-closed via context.aborted)
controls.gap: Code-level secret/PII scanning exists, but there is no malware/AV scanning of file attachments accepted by the chat API (attachmentValidator caps size at 25 MiB but does not scan content), and no signature/provenance verification of the MCP subprocess binaries the proxy spawns. The synth-executor sandbox (which would be the malicious-code-execution containment for SI-3) is excised from the launch branch.
controls.remediation: Add content scanning (ClamAV/equivalent) on uploaded attachments before persistence; verify checksums/signatures of MCP server images at spawn time in mcp_manager.initialize_servers. Document the attachment threat model.

controls.title: System Monitoring
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/services/AuditLogService.ts + AuditLogStreamingService.ts (AUDIT_LOG_SINK=stdout|datadog|splunk|fluentd|s3 batched export of FlowAuditLog); services/openagentic-api/src/services/approval/auditLog.ts insertAuditRow (every tool call audited); metrics/index.js (Prometheus metrics incl. bootstrapStepDuration); config/fastify.config.ts (per-request structured logging + 5xx warn/error)
controls.gap: Telemetry and audit capture exist and can stream to an external SIEM, but there is no built-in anomaly/intrusion detection or automated alerting on security events (failed-auth spikes, repeated approval denials, DLP blocks). SECURITY.md claims 'anomaly alerts' but no code emits them — they are doc-aspirational. SI-4 for High expects detection of indicators of attack.
controls.remediation: Emit dedicated security-event metrics/counters (auth_failures_total, dlp_blocks_total, approval_denied_total) and ship example Prometheus alerting rules; wire failed-login/lockout counters (the audit-log-unify backlog references an auth_audit_log table not yet present).

controls.title: System Monitoring | Automated Response to Suspicious Events
controls.evidence: No code path takes automated defensive action on a detected suspicious event; rate-limiting in config/fastify.config.ts is a static per-user/IP throttle, not an adaptive response to attack indicators
controls.gap: There is no automated response (block/notify) tied to detected suspicious activity such as repeated denied mutating-tool approvals, RLS failures, or auth anomalies.
controls.remediation: Add an event-driven hook that, on threshold breach (e.g. N approval denials or auth failures per principal per window), quarantines the principal/session and emits an alert event to the audit sink.

controls.title: Security Alerts, Advisories, and Directives
controls.evidence: SECURITY.md defines the inbound vulnerability-reporting channel (hello@agenticwork.io, 48h ack)
controls.gap: Receiving/disseminating external security advisories (US-CERT/CISA) is an organizational process control, not implementable in this OSS codebase. The repo provides the intake channel; advisory subscription/dissemination is an org responsibility for the deploying agency.

controls.title: Security and Privacy Function Verification (fail-closed on bad config)
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/config/secrets.config.ts validateSecret() — on a missing OR placeholder ('change_me','changeme','password','default', etc.) required secret it calls generateSecureSecret() and RETURNS it (console.error only), unconditionally; loadSecrets() never passes a production flag; startup/01-secrets.ts catches and downgrades any error to a warn ('Server will continue starting')
controls.gap: The platform does NOT verify that security-critical configuration (JWT_SECRET, DB/Milvus/Minio creds) is valid before operating — it fails OPEN. A production boot with JWT_SECRET=change_me silently substitutes an ephemeral random secret and continues, which (a) lets the platform run with placeholder/insecure config undetected and (b) rotates the signing secret on every restart (invalidating all sessions/tokens). This is the live 'compose -change-me secrets' blocker.
controls.remediation: Make validateSecret fail closed in production: throw on missing/placeholder required secrets so the API refuses to boot (the exact upstream behavior). Make startup/01-secrets.ts a critical step (or let the throw propagate) so a misconfigured prod stack does not start with ephemeral creds.

controls.title: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity (audit immutability)
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/prisma/migrations/20260218_audit_immutable/migration.sql (BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE triggers calling prevent_audit_modification() on admin_audit_log, user_query_audit, credential_exchange_audit, synth_capability_audit); src/__tests__/architecture/audit-log-append-only.source-regression.test.ts (source-cage: no toolCallAuditLog.delete*, only the guarded updateMany WHERE decision='pending' in services/approval/auditLog.ts)
controls.gap: The approval-gate's primary audit table tool_call_audit_log (admin schema, schema.prisma:1595) is NOT covered by the DB immutability trigger — only an app-level source-regression cage protects it, which prisma db push and any direct DB/role with table access can bypass. The other 4 tables get DB-enforced immutability; this one does not. Also no integrity verification (checksums/signatures) of deployed binaries or container images at boot — the entrypoint runs `prisma db push` but verifies no artifact integrity.
controls.remediation: Extend the immutable migration to add a partial trigger on admin.tool_call_audit_log that blocks DELETE and blocks UPDATE except the single pending->{approved,denied,timed_out} transition (BEFORE UPDATE trigger allowing the change only WHEN OLD.decision='pending'), matching the app-level guard. Add image-digest pinning + verification for service/MCP images.

controls.title: Software/Information Integrity | Integrity Checks (boot-time verification)
