controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/startup/13-milvus-collection-probe.ts (probes mcp_tools/agents collection sizes; MILVUS_BOOT_GATE_REQUIRED=true makes it critical→pod won't go ready); services/openagentic-api/docker-entrypoint.sh (fail-closed waits for Milvus/Redis/MCP-proxy/embedding, then `prisma db push` aborts boot on failure); startup/index.ts runStartup (critical steps process.exit(1))
controls.gap: Boot-time integrity gating exists for data-plane dependency readiness and (optionally) vector-collection population, but there is no cryptographic integrity check of the software itself (no image-digest/signature verification, no SBOM attestation check). The Milvus boot gate also defaults to non-critical (warn-and-continue) unless MILVUS_BOOT_GATE_REQUIRED is set.
controls.remediation: Pin container images by sha256 digest in helm/compose and add cosign/sigstore verification (admission policy) so only attested images run; default the Milvus boot gate to required in the production helm values.

controls.title: Information Input Validation
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/config/fastify.config.ts (FASTIFY_BODY_LIMIT_BYTES=100MiB ceiling + custom JSON parser → 400 on malformed body; CORS exact-origin allowlist); routes/chat/pipeline/dlp-scan.stage.ts (blocks credential/PII input pre-LLM, fail-closed); routes/setup.ts complete() (email/password/host field validation); routes/v1/webhooks.ts:122 (Slack HMAC signature verify); middleware/rls-context.ts (parameterized RLS context, fail-closed)
controls.gap: Validation is inconsistent and one endpoint is exploitable: POST /api/setup/probe-ollama (routes/setup.ts:62) is INTENTIONALLY UNAUTHENTICATED and does fetch(`${host}/api/tags`) with host taken verbatim from the request body — only trim + trailing-slash strip, NO check that the target is a private Ollama host. This is a server-side request forgery (SSRF) primitive: an unauthenticated caller can probe internal services / cloud metadata (169.254.169.254) and read back HTTP status + JSON. Separately, most routes rely on TypeScript Body types rather than registered Fastify JSON schemas, so structural input validation is not enforced uniformly.
controls.remediation: Add an allowlist/denylist guard to probe-ollama: resolve the host, reject loopback/link-local/private/metadata IP ranges and non-http(s) schemes before fetch; consider requiring the magic-boot token. Adopt Fastify JSON-schema validation on mutating routes for uniform structural input validation.

controls.title: Error Handling (no sensitive info in errors; fail-closed)
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/routes/chat/index.ts:477 fastify.setErrorHandler; routes/chat/middleware/logging.middleware.ts:249 setErrorHandler(errorMiddleware); services/approval/auditAndGate.ts runAuditAndGate (MUTATING pending-INSERT failure returns {allowed:false, blockReason:'Approval audit unavailable; mutating call blocked'} — fails SAFE); config/secrets.config.ts maskSecret()/logSecrets() (secrets masked in logs); services/AuditLogStreamingService.ts (sink network errors caught + logged, never re-thrown)

controls.title: Information Output Filtering
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/services/secretRedaction.ts + utils/log-sanitizer.ts; services/openagentic-api/src/services/llm-providers/__tests__/OllamaProvider.outboundArgsSanitize.test.ts (outbound tool-arg sanitization); DLPScannerService scanPoint supports user_input (input side); UI sanitize.ts + SharedMarkdownRenderer.sanitizeSchema (output HTML sanitization / XSS)
controls.gap: Output filtering exists for secrets-in-logs and rendered-HTML XSS, and DLP scans user input, but there is no consistent DLP/redaction pass on the ASSISTANT/tool OUTPUT returned to the user or written to the audit preview — makePreview (services/approval/auditLog.ts) truncates to 500 chars but does not redact secrets/PII captured in tool args, so sensitive values in mutating-tool arguments are persisted to tool_call_audit_log.args/preview in cleartext.
controls.remediation: Run the secretRedaction/DLP scrubber over tool args before insertAuditRow persists them, and over assistant/tool output before it leaves the platform, mirroring the input-side dlp-scan stage.

controls.title: Memory Protection
controls.evidence: No application-layer memory-protection code; relies on the Node.js/V8 runtime, container, and host OS (DEP/ASLR) for execution-environment memory safeguards
controls.gap: Memory protection (ASLR/DEP/stack canaries) is provided by the managed runtime and underlying OS/container platform, not by this application code. For an OSS app this is an inherited/platform control, satisfied by the deploying agency's hardened base images and kernel settings.

controls.title: Human-in-the-Loop Approval Gate on Mutating Actions (integrity of mutating tool execution)
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/services/approval/auditAndGate.ts runAuditAndGate + auditMcpExecutionSeam (every tool call audited; MUTATING calls with gate ON persist 'pending', emit approval_required SSE, await ApprovalRegistry.waitFor, block on deny/timeout); approval/classifyTool.ts (READ vs MUTATING verb classifier, unknown→READ but exact mutating verb always wins); routes/chat/approval-gate.routes.ts (POST /api/approvals/:auditId/{approve,deny}, guarded single transition + registry.submit); server.ts:963 + :1118 register both route modules on the live path; __tests__/architecture/approval-audit-routes-mounted.source-regression.test.ts pins the wiring
controls.gap: Functionally complete and fail-safe (a failed pending-INSERT blocks the mutation; deny/timeout blocks). Residual weakness: the resume mechanism (ApprovalRegistry) is an in-process EventEmitter map explicitly SINGLE-REPLICA only — an awaiting approval does not survive an api restart or work across HA replicas (the audit row stays 'pending' but the awaiting promise is lost). Acceptable for the single-replica helm deploy but a High-baseline availability/HA concern.
controls.remediation: For HA, back the pending-approval wait/resume with a durable store (Redis pub/sub or DB polling on decision change) so a restarted or peer replica can resume an in-flight approval; reconcile orphaned 'pending' rows on boot.

controls.title: Baseline Configuration
controls.evidence: docker-compose.yml:28,46,69,91,107,490 (postgres pgvector:pg16, redis:7-alpine, etcd v3.5.18, minio RELEASE.2024-12-18, milvus v2.4.15, prom v2.54.1 — all version/date-tagged); services/openagentic-api/Dockerfile:13,129 (node:22-slim); services/openagentic-ui/Dockerfile:8,29,129 (node:20-slim + nginx:alpine)
controls.gap: A baseline config exists (compose + Dockerfiles + helm define a reproducible stack) but it is NOT immutably pinned: NO image is pinned by @sha256 digest anywhere (git grep '@sha256:' over Dockerfiles/compose/helm returns nothing). Floating tags ride the worst: docker-compose.yml:127,142 ollama/ollama:latest, :460 searxng/searxng:latest, UI Dockerfile:129 nginx:alpine, and every MCP/proxy Dockerfile defaults ARG PYTHON_TAG=latest (cgr.dev/chainguard/python:${PYTHON_TAG}). helm/openagentic/values.yaml:18-19 defaults image tag:latest with pullPolicy:IfNotPresent. A 'latest' baseline is not a baseline — two builds of the same source produce different running images, defeating CM-2 reproducibility and CM-2(2) automation/integrity.
controls.remediation: Pin every base image and every deployed image by digest. In each Dockerfile, replace floating tags with FROM <image>@sha256:<digest> (or set the build-arg default to a digest, e.g. ARG PYTHON_TAG=<digest-ref>). In docker-compose.yml pin ollama/searxng/nginx/minio to a digest. In helm/values.yaml set a concrete tag (the release SHA) and pullPolicy: IfNotPresent, never 'latest'. Add a source-regression arch test that fails CI on any FROM line or compose image without an @sha256 digest, mirroring the existing no-hardcoded-* cages.

controls.title: Configuration Settings (secure baseline values)
controls.evidence: docker-compose.yml:276 JWT_SECRET:-openagentic-dev-jwt-secret-change-me, :277 SIGNING_SECRET:-...-change-me, :283 INTERNAL_API_KEY:-...-change-me, :280 ADMIN_SEED_PASSWORD:-changeme-on-first-login, :93-94 MINIO_ROOT_USER/PASSWORD: minioadmin/minioadmin (not even env-overridable). Counter-evidence (done right): :32 POSTGRES_PASSWORD:?set POSTGRES_PASSWORD in .env (compose-level required-var guard) and .env.example:8,48 ship REPLACE_ME_AT_INSTALL_TIME placeholders.
controls.gap: The shipped baseline config bakes insecure default secrets into the running stack. If an operator runs `docker compose up` without overriding these in .env, the API boots with a known JWT/SIGNING/INTERNAL key (anyone can forge internal-service and user tokens) and a known admin password. minio creds are hardcoded with no override path at all. POSTGRES_PASSWORD is correctly guarded with :? but the auth-critical secrets are not — they use :- soft defaults. This is the inverse of CM-6's 'secure-by-default established configuration'.
controls.remediation: Convert the auth-critical compose defaults from soft `:-placeholder` to required `:?` guards (e.g. JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:?set JWT_SECRET}, same for SIGNING_SECRET, INTERNAL_API_KEY, ADMIN_SEED_PASSWORD) so a bare `up` fails fast rather than booting with a forgeable key. Make MINIO_ROOT_USER/PASSWORD env-driven and required. Have the setup wizard (tools/setup) generate cryptographically random values into .env at install time (it already writes .env). Add an arch test forbidding 'change-me'/'changeme'/'minioadmin' literals in docker-compose.yml, paralleling no-hardcoded-colors-in-compose.source-regression.test.ts.

controls.title: Least Functionality — disabled features fail safe; the secrets fail-closed validator
controls.evidence: LAUNCH branch services/openagentic-api/src/config/secrets.config.ts:72 `function validateSecret(name, value, allowEmpty = false): string` — signature has NO prod parameter. On missing/placeholder secret it executes :89-95 generateSecureSecret()+console.error and RETURNS the ephemeral value (same for placeholder branch). loadSecrets():133-166 never passes any prod flag. Doc comment :63-71 literally states 'Logs a CRITICAL warning (never crashes) ... ensures the API server always starts'.
controls.gap: The secrets validator does NOT fail closed. In production a missing or 'change-me' JWT_SECRET / DATABASE_URL / API_KEY / MINIO key is silently replaced with a random per-restart value and boot continues. Combined with the CM-6 compose defaults, the platform will start with auto-generated or placeholder-derived auth material — exactly the insecure-by-default boot CM-7 (and SC/IA) forbid. Ephemeral JWT secrets also invalidate all sessions on every restart and make forged-token analysis non-deterministic.
controls.remediation: Make validateSecret fail closed in production: add a `prod` parameter that THROWS on missing/placeholder required secrets instead of generating a fallback, and have loadSecrets pass `isProduction` (NODE_ENV==='production') to every required-secret call while keeping dev/test ephemeral generation. This is exactly the hardened version that already exists in the enterprise overlay (working tree) at the same path — port it surgically.

controls.title: Least Functionality | Periodic Review / restrict unauthorized internal endpoints
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/routes/internal/agent-search.ts:88-95 — `if (!internalSecret) return reply.code(401)` (empty server-side secret rejects everything) then constant header compare; sibling internal routes (embed.ts, tool-search.ts) follow the same contract, asserted by routes/internal/__tests__/agent-search.test.ts:92-104 '401 when server-side secret is empty (fail-closed)' and embed.test.ts:7-8. services/openagentic-workflows/src/middleware/requireInternalKey.ts is the workflows-side equivalent.

controls.title: Least Functionality | Authorized Software — no-hardcoded-config / env-driven configuration cages
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/config/featureFlags.ts:38-52 requireEnv() crashes when a deploy-critical env var (cluster/namespace/tenant) is unset ('No fallback exists by design'); :65-83 every capability (ollamaEnabled, mcpProxyEnabled, approvalGateMutating, useRbacPrompt) is an env toggle resolved once at load. Guard cages on launch: src/__tests__/architecture/no-hardcoded-model-literals.source-regression.test.ts, no-defaultmodels-seeder, registry-sot-cage-no-default-models, no-hardcoded-router-floors, no-hardcoded-max-turns, plus docs/rules/no-hardcoded-models.md. config/service-discovery.ts:42-55 derives every endpoint from <SERVICE>_HOST/_PORT env, no hardcoded registry/tenant.

controls.title: Least Functionality | Workflow template engine env-exfil fail-safe (env reads disabled)
controls.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/services/WorkflowExecutionEngine.envExfil.test.ts:1-18 pins the sev-0 fix: {{env.X}} in a workflow node resolves ONLY from the engine-controlled context.variables allow-list; process.env is never read, so {{env.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY}} cannot exfil pod env. This is least-functionality applied to the user-authored template surface.

controls.title: Configuration Change Control
controls.evidence: .githooks/pre-commit:1-60 (staged-file secret/forbidden-name scanning) is the closest technical artifact; full CM-3 (CCB, change requests, impact analysis, approvals) is an organizational ATO process.
controls.gap: CM-3 is an org-process control (change request workflow, CCB approval, security impact analysis) not satisfiable by repo code. The pre-commit secret guard supports it but does not constitute change control.

controls.title: Access Restrictions for Change
controls.evidence: .githooks/pre-commit:1-60 blocks committing secrets/SECRETS-BACKUP.md/manifests.yaml; memory records main is branch-protected + CODEOWNERS-gated. In-repo evidence is limited to the pre-commit hook (which is advisory — requires `git config core.hooksPath .githooks` to be active).
controls.gap: Repository-side enforcement (branch protection, required reviewers, signed commits, CI gating on the no-hardcoded cages) lives in GitHub settings, not the codebase, so it cannot be substantiated from the launch branch alone. The pre-commit hook is local/opt-in and bypassable with --no-verify.
controls.remediation: Make the secret/no-hardcoded cages CI-required (run the architecture/*.source-regression.test.ts suite + a digest-pin check + the pre-commit secret patterns as a required GitHub Actions check on PRs to main), enforce signed commits + required reviews via branch protection, and document the CODEOWNERS map. These are configuration of the SCM, not code, but should be captured in the SSP as the CM-5 implementation.

controls.title: Baseline Configuration | Automation Support for Accuracy and Currency
controls.evidence: tools/sync-upstream.py (referenced in CLAUDE.md) reconciles the OSS baseline against upstream with PRESERVE/SKIP lists; the architecture/*.source-regression.test.ts cages enforce config invariants automatically.
controls.gap: No automated drift detection between the declared baseline (Dockerfiles/compose/helm) and what is actually deployed, and no digest-pin enforcement (see CM-2). The sync tool maintains source currency but does not attest the running image set matches an approved, hashed baseline.
controls.remediation: Add an SBOM + image-digest manifest generated at build (e.g. emit a lockfile of resolved @sha256 digests per release) and a CI/admission check that the deployed images match it. Pair with the CM-2 digest-pinning remediation so the baseline is verifiable end-to-end.

controls.title: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning
controls.evidence: git show oss-launch/a3-fedramp:.github/workflows (only oss-integrity.yml + sonar.yml exist); oss-integrity.yml:18 runs ONLY ./tools/verify-oss-integrity.sh (edition-flag + install-artifact presence — no CVE/dependency scan); package.json:lint:security points at ./scripts/scan-secrets.sh which does NOT exist on the launch branch (git log --all -- scripts/scan-secrets.sh returns nothing — dangling reference)
controls.gap: No automated vulnerability/dependency/container CVE scanning runs in CI on the launch branch. There is no trivy, no npm/pnpm audit, no Dependabot/Renovate (no .github/dependabot.yml), and no recurring (scheduled) scan. SECURITY.md states the only PR-time workflow is oss-integrity.yml 'which only reads source' — i.e. the launch branch self-documents that no CVE scan gates merges.
controls.remediation: Port the overlay's Security Scan workflow (gitleaks + trivy-fs CRITICAL/HIGH gate + recurring Mon-03:00 cron) verbatim, but FIX the npm-audit job: the repo is pnpm-only (pnpm-lock.yaml, no package-lock.json), so `npm audit` will not resolve the workspace — replace with `pnpm audit --audit-level=high` (or `pnpm audit --json | osv-scanner`). Add a .github/dependabot.yml for npm + pip + docker + github-actions ecosystems for continuous advisory monitoring.

controls.title: Update Vulnerabilities to Be Scanned (frequency / new advisories)
controls.evidence: No scheduled scan exists on the launch branch (git show oss-launch/a3-fedramp:.github/workflows/sonar.yml triggers only push/pull_request to main + workflow_dispatch — no `schedule:`; oss-integrity.yml likewise has no cron). FedRAMP High requires recurring scans that catch advisories filed AFTER code lands.
controls.gap: No recurring scan picks up NEW CVEs against dependency versions already merged to main. The launch branch has no mechanism to re-scan unchanged code as the advisory database updates.
controls.remediation: The overlay security-scan.yml carries `schedule: - cron: '0 3 * * 1'` (RA-5 recurring scan) — port it. Combine with Dependabot/Renovate for daily advisory diffing so newly-disclosed CVEs against pinned versions raise a finding without a code change.

controls.title: Breadth and Depth of Coverage (SCA across all ecosystems)
controls.evidence: Launch branch has no SCA at all (no audit/trivy/snyk anywhere in git ls-tree oss-launch/a3-fedramp). The repo spans TS/JS (pnpm workspaces) AND Python (services/mcps/oap-*-mcp, services/openagentic-synth, synth-executor) — neither ecosystem is scanned.
controls.gap: Even if the overlay security-scan.yml is ported, its npm-audit job covers only JS deps (and is mis-tooled for pnpm), and trivy-fs `skip-dirs` excludes node_modules — so it relies on lockfile manifests. Python dependency advisories (pip-audit/safety) are not covered by any job. Container-image layer scanning (vs filesystem scan) is also absent — see SA-11.
controls.remediation: Add pip-audit (or trivy with the python analyzer un-skipped) for the 5 Python services, and ensure trivy scans lockfiles (pnpm-lock.yaml, requirements.txt/pyproject) rather than skipping vendored dirs. Add per-image trivy `image` scans in the build pipeline (see SA-11 / RA-5 container coverage).

controls.title: Privileged Access (authenticated/container image scanning)
controls.evidence: git show oss-launch/a3-fedramp lists 10 Dockerfiles (services/*/Dockerfile) with NO scan step in any of them (services/openagentic-api/Dockerfile has no trivy/syft/audit line); there is no build-and-push workflow on the launch branch at all (only oss-integrity.yml + sonar.yml), so no built image is ever scanned before push.
controls.gap: No container image is scanned post-build for OS-package CVEs. The overlay's build-and-push.yml (the only image build/push pipeline) contains ZERO scan/sbom steps (grep -c trivy|syft|cosign|grype = 0) — so even the upstream image pipeline does not scan images. This is a genuine gap on BOTH branches, not merely a missing port.
controls.remediation: Add a `trivy image` (severity CRITICAL,HIGH, exit-code 1) step after each `docker build` in the image pipeline before `docker push`, and a cosign sign + attest step. The overlay security-scan.yml's trivy-fs job is filesystem-only and is the closest existing artifact to extend.

controls.title: Public Disclosure Program / Coordinated Vulnerability Reporting
controls.evidence: git show oss-launch/a3-fedramp:SECURITY.md:9-26 defines a private reporting channel (hello@agenticwork.io + @agentic-work), required report contents, a 48h acknowledgment and 5-business-day response commitment, plus in-scope/out-of-scope sections; .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/security_issue.yml exists on the launch branch.

controls.title: Developer Testing/Evaluation — Static Code Analysis (SAST)
controls.evidence: git show oss-launch/a3-fedramp:.github/workflows/sonar.yml runs SonarSource/sonarqube-scan-action@v5 with -Dsonar.qualitygate.wait=true on push/PR to main; sonar-project.properties (launch branch) sets projectKey/sources=services,tools,scripts,tests with exclusions. Additionally .eslintrc.js (launch) wires eslint-plugin-security ('plugin:security/recommended', security/detect-object-injection, security/detect-non-literal-regexp) + eslint-plugin-sonarjs.
controls.gap: Two material gaps. (1) The SonarQube scan targets a PRIVATE self-hosted instance (runs-on: openagentic-runners, reports to sonarqube-dev.agenticwork.io with secrets.SONAR_TOKEN) — an outside ATO assessor cannot observe results, and the workflow comment confirms Community Edition does NOT do PR decoration, so findings do not block merge unless the quality gate fails. (2) The eslint security rules are set to 'warn' (not 'error') and lint:eslint is NOT invoked by any CI workflow (grep of .github/workflows for lint:eslint/lint:security returns nothing) — so SAST findings from eslint never gate anything. There is no CodeQL anywhere on the launch branch.
controls.remediation: Promote security/* eslint rules to 'error' and add a CI job that runs `pnpm lint` (eslint + sonarjs) as a blocking gate. Add a GitHub-native CodeQL workflow (default-runner, public-visible SARIF) so the SAST result is assessor-observable and merge-blocking independent of the private SonarQube instance. Confirm the SonarQube quality gate is configured to fail on new Vulnerabilities/Security Hotspots, not just coverage.

controls.title: Developer Testing/Evaluation — SBOM (supply-chain attestation)
controls.evidence: Launch branch has no scripts/build.sh (git show oss-launch/a3-fedramp:scripts/build.sh = does not exist) and no syft/SBOM step in any Dockerfile or workflow (no spdx output anywhere). The overlay carries a policy test scripts/policy/sbom-ci-security.policy.test.ts asserting build.sh emits an SPDX-JSON SBOM via syft, and security-scan.yml has an `sbom` job (syft dir:. -o spdx-json), but neither the policy test, the workflow, nor build.sh exist on the launch branch.
controls.gap: No SBOM is generated for source tree or per-image. NOTE the SBOM control is only PARTIALLY satisfied even upstream: the overlay's scripts/build.sh contains NO syft/sbom step (grep returns empty), so the SA-11 policy test (sbom-ci-security.policy.test.ts S2-1) would FAIL against the actual overlay build.sh — the per-image SBOM the policy claims is not substantiated. Only the source-tree SBOM job in security-scan.yml is real.
controls.remediation: Port the security-scan.yml `sbom` job (source-tree SPDX-JSON via syft) — that part is real and works. For the per-image SBOM, ADD a syft step to the actual image build pipeline (it is missing upstream too); do not assume the overlay build.sh already has it. Then port scripts/policy/sbom-ci-security.policy.test.ts as the policy-as-code cage so removal regresses loudly.

controls.title: Flaw Remediation — written remediation SLA keyed to severity
controls.evidence: git show oss-launch/a3-fedramp:SECURITY.md contains only a reporting timeline (48h ack / 5-business-day response, lines 25-26) — grep for sev0/sev1/24h/72h/CRITICAL/HIGH/remediat returns NOTHING on the launch branch. The overlay SECURITY.md is also missing the SLA tiers (same grep empty), even though overlay security-scan.yml and the SBOM policy test reference 'the SLA windows defined in SECURITY.md (sev0/CRITICAL = 24h, sev1/HIGH = 72h)'.
controls.gap: The CI gate comments and the policy test assert a written severity-keyed remediation SLA (CRITICAL=24h, HIGH=72h) in SECURITY.md, but that SLA text does not actually exist in SECURITY.md on EITHER branch — so the SI-2 control the assessor reads is fabricated by the workflow comment. This is a claim-vs-substance gap, not a clean port.
controls.remediation: Add an explicit 'Flaw Remediation SLA' section to SECURITY.md with the severity tiers the CI references (sev0/CRITICAL ≤24h, sev1/HIGH ≤72h, sev2/MODERATE ≤30d, etc.). Then the security-scan.yml severity gate (CRITICAL,HIGH fail) and the sbom-ci-security.policy.test.ts SI-2 assertion become substantiated rather than dangling.

controls.title: Secret Scanning in CI and Pre-Commit (credential leakage detection)
controls.evidence: Launch branch active hook is .githooks/pre-commit (working tree core.hooksPath=.githooks): it does regex-based secret blocking (AWS AKIA, ya29 OAuth, awc_ keys, BEGIN PRIVATE-KEY, azureClientSecret). SECURITY.md:43 documents this. The alternate scripts/pre-commit runs `gitleaks protect --staged` but is OPT-IN (requires `git config core.hooksPath scripts/`) and is NOT the wired hook.
controls.gap: The wired pre-commit (.githooks) is hand-rolled regex coverage (5 patterns) — far narrower than a maintained scanner, and easily bypassed (git commit --no-verify, documented in the hook itself). The opt-in scripts/pre-commit references both .github/workflows/gitleaks.yml and a committed .gitleaks.toml baseline — NEITHER exists on the launch branch (git show ...:.gitleaks.toml = does not exist; no gitleaks.yml in workflows). So there is NO CI-side secret scan on the launch branch at all: a direct push to main (or any contributor's local without the opt-in hook) is unscanned for secrets.
controls.remediation: Port the gitleaks job from the overlay security-scan.yml (PR-diff scan on pull_request, working-tree no-git scan on push/schedule) so secret scanning runs in CI and gates merge regardless of local hooks. Add the committed .gitleaks.toml baseline that scripts/pre-commit already references. Optionally enable GitHub native secret scanning + push protection on the public repo.

controls.title: Risk Assessment (organizational risk-assessment process)
controls.evidence: No code artifact substantiates an organization-level risk-assessment process; this is a process/SSP control documented in the System Security Plan, not in the OSS codebase.
controls.remediation: Document in the SSP, out of band from the repo. The repo's technical contribution to RA-3 is the RA-5 scanning posture (above), which feeds the risk picture.

controls.title: Security Categorization
controls.evidence: Security categorization (FIPS-199 impact levels) is an SSP/authorization-boundary artifact; nothing in the OSS codebase implements or substantiates it.
controls.remediation: Document categorization in the SSP. Not a code control.

controls.title: Risk Response
controls.evidence: Risk-response decisions (accept/mitigate/transfer/avoid) are an organizational POA&M process; the repo's SECURITY.md disclosure flow and (once ported) CI severity gates are inputs, but the control itself is procedural.
controls.remediation: Track in a POA&M; the CI gate exit-codes (sev0/sev1 fail) provide the technical trigger feeding the risk-response process once security-scan.yml is ported.

nist.familyName: System & Services Acquisition / Assessment, Authorization & Monitoring (NIST 800-53 Rev5 High baseline)

controls.title: Control Assessments (continuous source-regression suite)
controls.evidence: oss-launch/a3-fedramp:services/openagentic-api/src/__tests__/architecture/ — 127 *.source-regression.test.ts files (e.g. audit-log-append-only.source-regression.test.ts:1, approval-audit-routes-mounted.source-regression.test.ts:1, docker-entrypoint-runs-migrations.source-regression.test.ts:1). vitest.config.ts:15-17 include 'src/**/*.test.ts' so the cages are runnable; package.json:test='vitest'.
controls.gap: The 127-test architecture/source-regression suite is the technical control-assessment evidence (each test pins a security/correctness invariant in source), but it is NOT executed by any CI lane. .github/workflows/oss-integrity.yml only runs the verify-oss-integrity.sh sanity shell; .github/workflows/sonar.yml explicitly states 'No deps install / no coverage step yet'. An assessment that never runs in the pipeline is not a continuous assessment — it can silently rot or be bypassed on merge to main.
controls.remediation: Add a CI job (on pull_request to main + push) that runs `pnpm -r test` (at minimum the openagentic-api vitest suite incl. the architecture cages) and fails the merge on any failure. Gate branch protection on it.

controls.title: Continuous Monitoring (recurring vuln + dependency scanning)
controls.evidence: oss-launch/a3-fedramp has only two workflows: .github/workflows/oss-integrity.yml (sanity shell) and .github/workflows/sonar.yml (SQ scan, no deps/coverage). No scheduled/recurring security scan is present on the launch branch (`git show oss-launch/a3-fedramp:.github/workflows/security-scan.yml` → does not exist).
controls.gap: No continuous/recurring monitoring of dependency CVEs or secrets on the launch branch. SonarQube runs only on push/PR and against a self-hosted ARC pool that an outside OSS contributor cannot trigger; there is no scheduled re-scan to catch advisories filed against versions already on main.
controls.remediation: Port the overlay security-scan.yml (it carries a `schedule: cron '0 3 * * 1'` recurring RA-5 scan) and retarget `runs-on: openagentic-runners` to `ubuntu-latest` for OSS portability.

controls.title: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning (dependency/image CVE scan)
controls.evidence: Launch branch has lockfiles (oss-launch/a3-fedramp:pnpm-lock.yaml, per-service package-lock.json, services/mcps/oap-*-mcp/requirements.txt) so deps are resolvable, but NO scanner runs against them. No trivy/npm-audit/grype config exists on the launch branch; sonar.yml has no audit step.
controls.gap: Nothing scans dependencies or container images for known CVEs and fails the build. FedRAMP High RA-5 requires recurring authenticated vuln scanning with severity-based remediation SLAs.
controls.remediation: Port the overlay security-scan.yml, which runs trivy-fs (severity CRITICAL,HIGH, exit-code 1) + `npm audit --audit-level=high` + gitleaks. Map its sev0/sev1 gates to the SECURITY.md SLA windows. Retarget the runner label to ubuntu-latest.

controls.title: Developer Testing and Evaluation (SBOM + security cages)
controls.evidence: oss-launch/a3-fedramp:services/openagentic-api/src/__tests__/architecture/no-api-vendored-sdk.source-regression.test.ts:1 (supply-chain cage: no hand-vendored SDK), audit-log-append-only.source-regression.test.ts (immutability cage). vitest.config.ts:31-44 wires an 80% coverage gate. These are real developer security/evaluation testing.
controls.gap: No SBOM is generated for the source tree or per-image — `git show oss-launch/a3-fedramp:.github/workflows/security-scan.yml` and `scripts/build.sh` both do not exist on the launch branch (grep for sbom/syft/spdx/cyclonedx returns nothing). FedRAMP/EO-14028 expect an attestable component manifest per build artifact. Also the 127 evaluation cages are not CI-gated (see CA-2).
controls.remediation: Port the overlay security-scan.yml `sbom` job (syft → SPDX-JSON, uploaded as an artifact) so every PR carries a component manifest; add a per-image SBOM at build time. Also CI-run the architecture suite.

controls.title: Developer Configuration Management (lockfiles, pinned deps, version baseline)
controls.evidence: oss-launch/a3-fedramp:pnpm-lock.yaml (root), services/openagentic-api/package-lock.json + pnpm-lock.yaml, services/openagentic-ui/package-lock.json, services/openagentic-workflows/package-lock.json, tools/setup/package-lock.json — JS deps are fully locked. version.json:1-13 is an explicit per-component version baseline (platform/api/ui/mcpProxy all pinned to 1.0.0). Docker base images are tag-pinned (services/openagentic-api/Dockerfile:1 `FROM node:22-slim`; oap-aws-mcp/Dockerfile uses cgr.dev/chainguard hardened base).
controls.gap: Python dependencies are NOT pinned and have no lockfile: services/openagentic-mcp-proxy/requirements.txt and services/mcps/oap-*-mcp/requirements.txt use floating ranges (e.g. `fastapi>=0.104.0,<1.0.0`, `boto3>=1.34.0`) with no hash-pinning and no requirements.lock / pip-compile output — builds are non-reproducible and a malicious-version-bump window is open. Docker base images are tag-pinned but NOT digest-pinned (`node:22-slim` / `python:3.11-slim`, no @sha256), so the base layer is mutable.
controls.remediation: Add hash-pinned Python lockfiles (pip-compile --generate-hashes → requirements.lock, install with --require-hashes) for the mcp-proxy and each MCP. Digest-pin all Dockerfile FROM lines (FROM node:22-slim@sha256:...).

controls.title: Flaw Remediation (advisory-driven pipeline fail with SLA)
controls.evidence: oss-launch/a3-fedramp:SECURITY.md defines a vuln-disclosure path and supported-versions table but does NOT define remediation SLA tiers on the launch branch; no pipeline gate enforces them. The overlay security-scan.yml references 'the SLA windows defined in SECURITY.md (sev0/CRITICAL = 24h, sev1/HIGH = 72h)' but that workflow and those SLA tiers are absent from the launch branch.
controls.gap: No automated flaw-remediation gate: nothing fails the pipeline on a known CRITICAL/HIGH advisory, and the launch SECURITY.md lacks the severity→SLA mapping the gate should enforce.
controls.remediation: Port security-scan.yml (its trivy/npm-audit jobs FAIL on CRITICAL/HIGH = the enforcement mechanism) AND add the sev0=24h / sev1=72h SLA tier table to the launch SECURITY.md so the gate has a documented basis.

controls.title: Development Process, Standards, and Tools (lint/format/quality gates)
controls.evidence: oss-launch/a3-fedramp:package.json:scripts wires lint (eslint --max-warnings 0), lint:types (tsc --noEmit), lint:circular (madge), lint:dead (knip), lint:deps (dependency-cruiser). sonar-project.properties:1 configures SonarQube (projectKey=openagentic, coverage via lcov). .dependency-cruiser.js + .eslintrc.js present.
controls.gap: Two defects: (1) `package.json` lint:security points to `./scripts/scan-secrets.sh` which does NOT exist on the launch branch (`git ls-tree oss-launch/a3-fedramp scripts/` shows only postgres-init/pre-commit/prometheus/searxng) — the security-lint target is broken. (2) None of these lint/quality scripts are run in CI; sonar.yml runs SQ but with no deps install/coverage, so the SQ quality gate evaluates against zero coverage.
controls.remediation: Restore scripts/scan-secrets.sh (or repoint lint:security to the gitleaks step), and add a CI lane that runs `pnpm lint` + tests with coverage so the SonarQube quality gate is meaningful.

controls.title: Supply Chain Controls and Processes (secret-injection / build chain)
controls.evidence: oss-launch/a3-fedramp:.githooks/pre-commit:1 blocks committed secrets (AWS keys, private keys, OAuth tokens) with rename-aware diff scanning; SECURITY.md documents 'Container build chain' (no PR-time image build/push from the public repo; main is branch-protected). version.json provides a release/changelog provenance record.
controls.gap: The pre-commit hook is a LOCAL gate only — there is no CI-side secret scan on the launch branch (gitleaks lives only in the overlay security-scan.yml), so a contributor who bypasses the local hook is not caught server-side. No SBOM / build attestation accompanies released artifacts (see SA-11).
controls.remediation: Port the gitleaks job from security-scan.yml as a server-side secret gate on every PR, and add SBOM generation to the release path.

controls.title: Component Authenticity / Build Provenance (signed images, attestation)
controls.evidence: No cosign/SLSA/in-toto/sigstore configuration anywhere on the launch branch (grep for cosign|slsa|provenance|attest returns nothing). The overlay build-and-push.yml builds+pushes images but performs NO signing or provenance attestation and is itself enterprise-leaky (GCP GAR registry, plus Flowise/code-manager/code-runner = the excised Code Mode services).
controls.gap: Released container images are neither signed nor accompanied by a build-provenance attestation, so consumers cannot verify authenticity/integrity of the artifacts — a hard requirement for a High supply-chain posture (EO-14028 / SLSA).
controls.remediation: Add a signing + provenance step to the OSS release build (cosign sign + SLSA provenance / `docker buildx --attest type=provenance,type=sbom`). Do NOT port build-and-push.yml wholesale — it references GCP-specific registry/WIF secrets and the removed Code Mode services (openagentic-manager, openagenticcode-runner, flowise); author a clean OSS publish workflow instead.

controls.title: Baseline Configuration (CI runner / config-integrity guard)
controls.evidence: Overlay has .github/workflows/runner-guard.yml (rejects GitHub-hosted runner labels via tools/verify-self-hosted-runners.sh) but it is absent on the launch branch (`git show oss-launch/a3-fedramp:.github/workflows/runner-guard.yml` → does not exist; tools/verify-self-hosted-runners.sh also absent).
controls.gap: No guard pins the CI execution baseline. For the public OSS edition this is arguably the inverse concern: the launch branch's remaining workflows (sonar.yml) target `runs-on: openagentic-runners` (a private self-hosted ARC pool an external fork cannot use), so those lanes silently no-op for outside contributors.
controls.remediation: For the OSS edition, retarget public CI lanes to ubuntu-latest GitHub-hosted runners so forks/PRs actually execute the gates; the enterprise runner-guard is org-process and need not ship in OSS.

controls.title: Vulnerability Disclosure / Public Reporting Channel
controls.evidence: oss-launch/a3-fedramp:SECURITY.md — private reporting channel (hello@agenticwork.io + @agent-work), 48h acknowledgment / 5-business-day response commitment, defined in-scope/out-of-scope, required report contents. .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/security_issue.yml provides a structured intake; CONTRIBUTING.md directs security issues to the private channel ('please report privately to hello@agenticwork.io').

controls.title: Plan of Action and Milestones (POA&M)
controls.evidence: POA&M is an organizational/process artifact maintained outside the code repository as part of the ATO package; nothing in an OSS source tree substantiates it. The in-repo KNOWN_VIOLATIONS_hardcoded_models.txt + architecture-test exception lists are the closest technical analog but are not a control-level POA&M.

controls.title: Authorization (ATO sign-off)
controls.evidence: Authorization is a management decision by an Authorizing Official, recorded in the ATO package — not a code-substantiated control in an OSS repo.

controls.title: Acquisition Process (license / third-party component governance)
controls.evidence: oss-launch/a3-fedramp:LICENSE + NOTICE + .licenserc.yaml (license-header policy) govern component licensing; lockfiles (pnpm-lock.yaml, package-lock.json) fully enumerate acquired JS components with resolved versions/integrity hashes. version.json records the assembled component baseline.

refutedImplemented.title: Session Authenticity / Inter-Service Trust
refutedImplemented.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/middleware/unifiedAuth.ts:113-114 (crypto.timingSafeEqual on the internal service secret, length-checked first); services/openagentic-api/src/utils/internalKeyReader.ts (single SoT internal key from projected Secret with mtime hot-reload for rotation); services/openagentic-proxy/src/middleware/auth.ts:28-31 (service-to-service Bearer + X-Agent-Proxy marker, else delegates token validation to the API /api/auth/validate-token)

Hardening opportunity (not a gap): the openagentic-proxy internal-key path (auth.ts:30) uses a plain === string compare for the Bearer value rather than timingSafeEqual; could be tightened, and the trust is bearer-token not mTLS (see SC-8).

refutedImplemented.title: Human-in-the-Loop Approval Gate on Mutating Actions (integrity of mutating tool execution)
refutedImplemented.evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/services/approval/auditAndGate.ts runAuditAndGate + auditMcpExecutionSeam (every tool call audited; MUTATING calls with gate ON persist 'pending', emit approval_required SSE, await ApprovalRegistry.waitFor, block on deny/timeout); approval/classifyTool.ts (READ vs MUTATING verb classifier, unknown→READ but exact mutating verb always wins); routes/chat/approval-gate.routes.ts (POST /api/approvals/:auditId/{approve,deny}, guarded single transition + registry.submit); server.ts:963 + :1118 register both route modules on the live path; __tests__/architecture/approval-audit-routes-mounted.source-regression.test.ts pins the wiring
refutedImplemented.gap: Functionally complete and fail-safe (a failed pending-INSERT blocks the mutation; deny/timeout blocks). Residual weakness: the resume mechanism (ApprovalRegistry) is an in-process EventEmitter map explicitly SINGLE-REPLICA only — an awaiting approval does not survive an api restart or work across HA replicas (the audit row stays 'pending' but the awaiting promise is lost). Acceptable for the single-replica helm deploy but a High-baseline availability/HA concern.
refutedImplemented.remediation: For HA, back the pending-approval wait/resume with a durable store (Redis pub/sub or DB polling on decision change) so a restarted or peer replica can resume an in-flight approval; reconcile orphaned 'pending' rows on boot.
